translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of Jeremiah the son of Hilkijah, of the priests that were in Anathoth in the land of Benjamin:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to whom the word of Jehovah came in the days of Josiah the son of Amon, king of Judah, in the thirteenth year of his reign;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: it came also in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah, unto the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah the son of Josiah, king of Judah, unto the carrying away of Jerusalem captive, in the fifth month.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee; and before thou camest forth out of the womb I hallowed thee, I appointed thee a prophet unto the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, Alas, Lord Jehovah! behold, I cannot speak; for I am a child.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah said unto me, Say not, I am a child; for thou shalt go to whomsoever I shall send thee, and whatsoever I command thee thou shalt speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not afraid of them; for I am with thee to deliver thee, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah put forth his hand and touched my mouth; and Jehovah said unto me, Behold, I have put my words in thy mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, I have this day set thee over the nations and over the kingdoms, to pluck up, and to break down, and to destroy, and to overthrow, to build and to plant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to me, saying, Jeremiah, what seest thou? And I said, I see a rod of an almond-tree.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, Thou hast well seen; for I am watchful over my word to perform it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to me the second time, saying, What seest thou? And I said, I see a seething-pot, and its face is from the north.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, Out of the north shall evil break forth upon all the inhabitants of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, I am calling all the families of the kingdoms of the north, saith Jehovah, and they shall come, and they shall set every one his throne at the entering of the gates of Jerusalem, and against all the walls thereof round about, and against all the cities of Judah:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will pronounce my judgments against them for all their wickedness, in that they have forsaken me, and have burned incense unto othergods, and worshipped the works of their own hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, therefore, gird up thy loins, and arise, and speak unto them all that I shall command thee: be not dismayed at them, lest I cause thee to be dismayed before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I, behold, I appoint thee this day as a strong city, and an iron pillar, and brazen walls, against the whole land; against the kings of Judah, against its princes, against its priests, and against the people of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall fight against thee, but they shall not prevail against thee: for I am with thee, saith Jehovah, to deliver thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and cry in the ears of Jerusalem, saying, Thus saith Jehovah: I remember for thee the kindness of thy youth, the love of thine espousals, when thou wentest after me in the wilderness, in a land not sown.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Israel was holiness unto Jehovah, the first-fruits of his increase: all that devour him are guilty; evil shall come upon them, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear the word of Jehovah, house of Jacob, and all the families of the house of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: What injustice have your fathers found in me, that they are gone far from me, and have walked after vanity, and become vain?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said not, Where is Jehovah, that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, that led us in the wilderness, in a land of deserts and of pits, in a land of drought and of the shadow of death, in a land that no one passeth through, and where no man dwelleth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I brought you into a fruitful land, to eat the fruit thereof and the good thereof; and ye entered and defiled my land, and made my heritage an abomination.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The priests said not, Where is Jehovah? and they that handled the law knew me not; and the shepherds transgressed against me; and the prophets prophesied by Baal, and walked after [things that] do not profit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore will I yet plead with you, saith Jehovah, and with your children's children will I plead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For pass over to the isles of Chittim, and see; and send unto Kedar, and consider diligently, and see if there have been such a thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hath a nation changed [its]gods? and they are nogods; — but my people have changed their glory for that which doth not profit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be astonished, ye heavens, at this, and shudder; be amazed very much, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my people have committed two evils: they have forsaken me, the fountain of living waters, to hew them out cisterns, broken cisterns that hold no water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is Israel a bondman? Is he a home-born [slave]? Why is he become a spoil?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The young lions roared against him, they gave forth their voice, and they made his land desolate: his cities are burned, without inhabitant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even the children of Noph and Tahapanes have fed on the crown of thy head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou not procured this unto thyself, in that thou hast forsaken Jehovah thyGod, at the time he was leading thee in the way?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, what hast thou to do with the way of Egypt, to drink the waters of Shihor? And what hast thou to do with the way of Assyria, to drink the waters of the River?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine own wickedness chastiseth thee, and thy backslidings reprove thee: know then and see that it is an evil thing and bitter that thou hast forsaken Jehovah thyGod, and that my fear is not in thee, saith the Lord, Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For of old thou hast broken thy yoke, [and] burst thy bands; and thou saidst, I will not serve. For upon every high hill, and under every green tree, thou bowest down, playing the harlot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I, — I had planted thee a noble vine, wholly a right seed; how then art thou turned into the degenerate shoots of a strange vine unto me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For though thou wash thee with nitre, and take thee much potash, thine iniquity is marked before me, saith the Lord Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How sayest thou, I am not defiled, I have not gone after the Baals? See thy way in the valley, acknowledge what thou hast done — a swift dromedary traversing her ways!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — a wild ass, used to the wilderness, that snuffeth up the wind in her desire! In her ardour, who shall turn her away? All they that seek her will not weary themselves; in her month they shall find her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Withhold thy foot from being unshod, and thy throat from thirst. But thou saidst, There is no hope; no, for I love strangers, and after them will I go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a thief is ashamed when he is found, so shall the house of Israel be ashamed — they, their kings, their princes, and their priests, and their prophets —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying to a stock, Thou art my father; and to a stone, Thou hast brought me forth; for they have turned the back unto me, and not the face; and in the time of their trouble they will say, Arise, and save us!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where then are thygods that thou hast made for thyself? let them arise, if they can save thee in the time of thy trouble: for as the number of thy cities, are thygods, O Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore would ye contend with me? Ye all have transgressed against me, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In vain have I smitten your children: they received no correction. Your own sword hath devoured your prophets, like a destroying lion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O generation, mark ye the word of Jehovah. Have I been a wilderness unto Israel, or a land of thick darkness? Wherefore say my people, We have dominion; we will come no more unto thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth a virgin forget her ornaments, a bride her attire? But my people have forgotten me days without number.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How dost thou trim thy way to seek love! Therefore hast thou also accustomed thy ways to wickedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, in thy skirts is found the blood of the souls of the innocent poor, whom thou didst not encounter breaking in, but [it is found] upon all these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou sayest, Indeed I am innocent; his anger will turn from me. Behold, I will enter into judgment with thee because thou sayest, I have not sinned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why dost thou gad about so much, and change thy way? Thou shalt also be brought to shame by Egypt, as thou wast brought to shame by Assyria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt indeed go forth from her with thy hands upon thy head; for Jehovah hath rejected those thou confidest in, and thou shalt not prosper by them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say, If a man put away his wife, and she go from him, and become another man's, shall he return unto her again? Would not that land be utterly polluted? But thou hast committed fornication with many lovers; yet return to me, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift up thine eyes unto the heights and see, where hast thou not been lain with? In the ways hast thou sat for them, as an Arab in the wilderness; and thou hast polluted the land with thy fornications and with thy wickedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the showers have been withholden, and there hath been no latter rain; but thou hast a harlot's forehead, thou refusest to be ashamed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou not from this time cry unto me, My father, thou art the guide of my youth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will he keep [his anger] for ever? Will he preserve it perpetually? Behold, thou hast spoken and hast done evil things, and thou art [so] determined.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me in the days of Josiah the king, Hast thou seen what backsliding Israel hath done? She hath gone up upon every high mountain and under every green tree, and there hath committed fornication.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, After she hath done all these [things], she will return unto me; but she returned not. And her sister Judah, the treacherous, saw [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I saw that when for all the causes wherein backsliding Israel committed adultery I had put her away, and given her a bill of divorce, yet the treacherous Judah, her sister, feared not, but went and committed fornication also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass through the lightness of her fornication that she polluted the land, and committed adultery with stones and with stocks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And even for all this her treacherous sister Judah hath not returned unto me with her whole heart, but with falsehood, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, Backsliding Israel hath shewn herself more just than treacherous Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and proclaim these words toward the north, and say, Return, thou backsliding Israel, saith Jehovah: I will not make my face dark upon you; for I am merciful, saith Jehovah; I will not keep [anger] for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only acknowledge thine iniquity, that thou hast transgressed against Jehovah thyGod, and hast turned thy ways hither and thither to the strangers under every green tree; and ye have not hearkened to my voice, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Return, backsliding children, saith Jehovah; for I am a husband unto you, and I will take you, one of a city, and two of a family, and I will bring you to Zion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give you shepherds according to my heart, and they shall feed you with knowledge and understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when ye are multiplied in the land and become fruitful, in those days, saith Jehovah, they shall say no more, Ark of the covenant of Jehovah! neither shall it come to mind, nor shall they remember it, nor shall they visit [it]; neither shall it be done any more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time they shall call Jerusalem the throne of Jehovah; and all the nations shall be gathered unto it, to the name of Jehovah, to Jerusalem; and they shall no more walk after the stubbornness of their evil heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days the house of Judah shall walk with the house of Israel; and they shall come together out of the land of the north, to the land which I caused your fathers to inherit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as for me, I said, How shall I put thee among the children, and give thee the pleasant land, the goodly inheritance of the hosts of the nations? And I said, Thou shalt call me, My father; and shalt not turn away from following me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely [as] a woman treacherously departeth from her companion, so have ye dealt treacherously with me, O house of Israel, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A voice is heard upon the heights, the weeping supplications of the children of Israel; for they have perverted their way, they have forgotten Jehovah theirGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Return, backsliding children; I will heal your backslidings. ...Behold, we come unto thee; for thou art Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Truly in vain [is salvation looked for] from the hills, [and] the multitude of mountains; truly in Jehovah ourGod is the salvation of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But shame hath devoured the labour of our fathers from our youth; their flocks and their herds, their sons and their daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We lie down in our shame, and our confusion covereth us; for we have sinned against Jehovah ourGod, we and our fathers, from our youth even unto this day, and have not hearkened to the voice of Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou wilt return, O Israel, saith Jehovah, return unto me; and if thou wilt put away thine abominations out of my sight, then shalt thou not be a wanderer;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — and thou shalt in truth, in justice, and in righteousness swear, [As] Jehovah liveth! and the nations shall bless themselves in him, and in him shall they glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah to the men of Judah and Jerusalem: Break up for you a fallow ground, and sow not among thorns.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Circumcise yourselves for Jehovah, and take away the foreskins of your heart, ye men of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem; lest my fury come forth like fire and burn, and there be none to quench it, because of the evil of your doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Declare ye in Judah, and cause it to be heard in Jerusalem, and say, ...and blow the trumpet in the land, cry aloud and say, Assemble yourselves, and let us enter into the fenced cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Set up a banner toward Zion; take to flight, stay not! For I am bringing evil from the north, and a great destruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lion is come up from his thicket, the destroyer of the nations is on his way; he is gone forth from his place, to make thy land desolate; thy cities shall be laid waste, without inhabitant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this, gird you with sackcloth, lament and howl! for the fierce anger of Jehovah is not turned away from us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass in that day, saith Jehovah, that the heart of the king shall perish, and the heart of the princes; and the priests shall be astonished, and the prophets shall be amazed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, Alas, Lord Jehovah! surely thou hast greatly deceived this people and Jerusalem, saying, Ye shall have peace; whereas the sword reacheth unto the soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time shall it be said to this people and to Jerusalem, A hot wind [cometh] from the heights in the wilderness, on the way of the daughter of my people, not for fanning, nor for cleansing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wind more vehement than that shall come from me: now will I also pronounce judgments against them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he cometh up as clouds, and his chariots are as a whirlwind; his horses are swifter than eagles. Woe unto us! for we are destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wash thy heart, Jerusalem, from wickedness, that thou mayest be saved. How long shall thy vain thoughts lodge within thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a voice declareth from Dan, and publisheth affliction from mount Ephraim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Inform the nations; behold, make Jerusalem to hear: Besiegers come from a far country, and raise their voice against the cities of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As keepers of a field are they against her round about; for she hath been rebellious against me, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy way and thy doings have procured these [things] unto thee; this is thy wickedness, yea, it is bitter, yea, it reacheth unto thy heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My bowels! my bowels! I am in travail! [Oh,] the walls of my heart! My heart maketh a noise in me; I cannot hold my peace: for thou hearest, my soul, the sound of the trumpet, the clamour of war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Destruction upon destruction is proclaimed; for the whole land is wasted: suddenly are my tents laid waste, my curtains, in a moment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long shall I see the standard, [and] hear the sound of the trumpet?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my people is foolish, they have not known me; they are sottish children, and they have no intelligence; they are wise to do evil, but to do good they have no knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I beheld the earth, and lo, it was waste and empty; and the heavens, and they had no light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I beheld the mountains, and lo, they trembled, and all the hills shook violently.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I beheld, and lo, man was not, and all the fowl of the heavens were fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I beheld, and lo, the fruitful land was a wilderness, and all the cities thereof were broken down, before Jehovah, before his fierce anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: The whole land shall be a desolation; but I will not make a full end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this shall the earth mourn, and the heavens above be black; because I have spoken it, I have purposed it, and will not repent, neither will I turn back therefrom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the noise of the horsemen and bowmen, every city fleeth; they go into the thickets, and climb up upon the rocks: every city is forsaken and no man dwelleth therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And thou, wasted one, what wilt thou do? Though thou clothest thyself with crimson, though thou deckest thee with ornaments of gold, though thou rendest thine eyes with paint, in vain dost thou make thyself fair: [thy] lovers despise thee, they seek thy life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I hear a voice, as of a woman in travail, anguish as of her that bringeth forth her first child, the voice of the daughter of Zion: she moaneth, she spreadeth forth her hands, [saying], Woe unto me! for my soul faileth because of murderers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Run ye to and fro through the streets of Jerusalem, and see now, and know, and seek in the broadways thereof, if ye can find a man, if there be [any] that doeth justice, that seeketh fidelity; and I will pardon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if they say, [As] Jehovah liveth! surely they swear falsely.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, are not thine eyes upon fidelity? Thou hast smitten them, but they are not sore; thou hast consumed them, they have refused to receive correction: they have made their faces harder than a rock; they have refused to return.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, Surely these are the wretched ones, they are foolish; for they know not the way of Jehovah, the judgment of theirGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will go unto the great men, and will speak unto them; for they know the way of Jehovah, the judgment of theirGod; but these have altogether broken the yoke, have burst the bonds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore a lion out of the forest shall slay them, a wolf of the evenings shall waste them; the leopard lurketh against their cities, every one that goeth out thence is torn in pieces: for their transgressions are multiplied, their backslidings are increased.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore should I pardon thee? Thy children have forsaken me, and swear by them that are notGod. I have satiated them, and they have committed adultery, and they troop to the harlots' house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] well fed horses, they roam about, every one neigheth after his neighbour's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall I not visit for these things? saith Jehovah, and shall not my soul be avenged on such a nation as this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go up upon her walls, and destroy; but make not a full end; take away her battlements, for they are not Jehovah's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the house of Israel and the house of Judah have dealt very treacherously against me, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have denied Jehovah, and say, He is not; and evil shall not come upon us, nor shall we see sword nor famine;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the prophets shall become wind, and the word is not in them: thus shall it be done unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah, theGod of hosts: Because ye speak this word, behold, I will make my words in thy mouth fire, and this people wood, and it shall devour them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I bring a nation upon you from afar, house of Israel, saith Jehovah: it is a mighty nation, it is an ancient nation, a nation whose language thou knowest not, neither understandest thou what they say.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their quiver is as an open sepulchre; they are all mighty men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall eat up thy harvest and thy bread, they shall eat up thy sons and thy daughters, they shall eat up thy flocks and thy herds, they shall eat up thy vines and thy fig-trees; they shall destroy with the sword thy strong cities, wherein thou trustedst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless in those days, saith Jehovah, I will not make a full end with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when ye shall say, Wherefore hath Jehovah ourGod done all these things unto us? then shalt thou say to them, As ye have forsaken me, and served strangegods in your land, so shall ye serve strangers in a land that is not yours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Declare this in the house of Jacob, and publish it in Judah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear now this, O foolish and heartless people, who have eyes and see not; who have ears, and hear not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will ye not fear me? saith Jehovah. Will ye not tremble at my presence, who have set the sand a bound for the sea by a perpetual decree, and it shall not pass it? and its waves toss themselves, but they do not prevail; and they roar, yet can they not pass over it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this people hath a stubborn and a rebellious heart; they have turned aside and are gone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they say not in their heart, Let us now fear Jehovah ourGod, that giveth rain, both the early and the latter, in its season; who preserveth unto us the appointed weeks of harvest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your iniquities have turned away these things, and your sins have withholden from you what is good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For among my people are found wicked [men]: they lay wait, as fowlers stoop down; they set a trap, they catch men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a cage full of birds, so are their houses full of deceit: therefore they are become great, and have enriched themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are become fat, they shine, yea, they surpass in deeds of wickedness; they judge not the cause, the cause of the fatherless, and they prosper; and the right of the needy do they not adjudge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall I not visit for these things? saith Jehovah; shall not my soul be avenged on such a nation as this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An appalling and horrible thing is committed in the land:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the prophets prophesy falsehood, and the priests rule by their means; and my people love [to have it] so. But what will ye do in the end thereof?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Flee for safety, ye children of Benjamin, out of the midst of Jerusalem, and blow the trumpet in Tekoa, and set up a signal in Beth-haccerem; for evil appeareth out of the north, and a great destruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The comely and delicate one do I cut off, the daughter of Zion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shepherds with their flocks shall come unto her; they shall pitch [their] tents against her round about; they shall feed every one in his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Prepare war against her. Arise, and let us go up at noon. Woe unto us! for the day hath declined, for the shadows of the evening are lengthening.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, and let us go up by night, and let us destroy her palaces.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus hath Jehovah of hosts said: Hew ye down trees, and cast a mound against Jerusalem. She is the city to be visited; she is wholly oppression in the midst of her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a well poureth forth her waters, so she poureth forth her wickedness: violence and destruction are heard in her; before me continually are grief and wounds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be thou instructed, Jerusalem, lest my soul be alienated from thee; lest I make thee a desolation, a land not inhabited.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: They shall thoroughly glean like a vine the remnant of Israel: turn back thy hand, as a grape-gatherer unto the baskets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To whom shall I speak and testify, that they may hear? Behold, their ear is uncircumcised, and they cannot hearken: behold, the word of Jehovah is unto them a reproach; they have no delight in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I am full of the fury of Jehovah, I am weary with holding in. Pour it out upon the children in the street, and upon the assembly of young men together: for even the husband with the wife shall be taken; the aged with him [that is] full of days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their houses shall be turned unto others, [their] fields and wives together; for I will stretch out my hand upon the inhabitants of the land, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For from the least of them even unto the greatest of them, every one is given to covetousness; and from the prophet even unto the priest, every one dealeth falsely.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they have healed the breach of the daughter of my people lightly, saying, Peace, peace! when there is no peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are they ashamed that they have committed abomination? Nay, they are not at all ashamed, neither know they what it is to blush. Therefore they shall fall among them that fall; at the time that I visit them they shall stumble, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Stand in the ways and see, and ask for the ancient paths, which is the good way, and walk therein, and ye shall find rest for your souls. But they said, We will not walk [therein].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also I have set watchmen over you: — Hearken ye to the sound of the trumpet. But they said, We will not hearken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore hear, ye nations, and know, O assembly, what is among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, O earth: behold, I will bring evil upon this people, the fruit of their thoughts; for they have not hearkened unto my words, and as to my law, they have rejected it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To what purpose should there come to me incense from Sheba, and the sweet cane from a far country? Your burnt-offerings are not acceptable, nor are your sacrifices pleasing unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will lay stumbling-blocks before this people, and the fathers and the sons together shall fall over them; the neighbour and his friend shall perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, a people cometh from the north country, and a great nation is stirred up from the uttermost parts of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They lay hold on bow and spear; they are cruel, and have no mercy; their voice roareth like the sea; and they ride upon horses, set in array as a man for the battle, against thee, daughter of Zion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have heard the report thereof: our hands are grown feeble; anguish hath taken hold of us, pain as of a woman that travaileth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go not forth into the field, nor walk by the way; for [there is] the sword of the enemy, terror is on every side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Daughter of my people, gird thee with sackcloth, and roll thyself in ashes: make mourning, [as] for an only son — bitter lamentation; for the spoiler cometh suddenly upon us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have set thee among my people as an assayer, a fortress, that thou mayest know and try their way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are all the most rebellious of rebels, going about with slander: they are bronze and iron; they are all corrupters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The bellows are burned, the lead is consumed by the fire; they have melted, and melted in vain; and the bad are not plucked away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Reprobate silver shall they call them, for Jehovah hath rejected them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Stand in the gate of Jehovah's house, and proclaim there this word, and say, Hear ye the word of Jehovah, all Judah, that enter in at these gates to worship Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Amend your ways and your doings, and I will cause you to dwell in this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Confide ye not in words of falsehood, saying, Jehovah's temple, Jehovah's temple, Jehovah's temple is this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye thoroughly amend your ways and your doings, if ye really do justice between a man and his neighbour,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [if] ye oppress not the stranger, the fatherless, and the widow, and shed no innocent blood in this place, neither walk after othergods to your hurt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then will I cause you to dwell in this place, in the land that I gave to your fathers from of old even for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, ye confide in words of falsehood that cannot profit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What? steal, murder, and commit adultery, and swear falsely, and burn incense unto Baal, and walk after othergods whom ye know not...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then ye come and stand before me, in this house which is called by my name, and say, We are delivered, — in order to do all these abominations!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is this house, which is called by my name, a den of robbers in your eyes? Even I, behold, I have seen it, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For go now unto my place which was in Shiloh, where I caused my name to dwell at the first, and see what I did to it, for the wickedness of my people Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, because ye have done all these works, saith Jehovah, and I spoke unto you, rising up early and speaking, and ye heard not, and I called you, and ye answered not;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will even do unto the house which is called by my name, wherein ye trust, and unto the place which I gave to you and to your fathers, as I have done to Shiloh;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will cast you out of my sight, as I have cast out all your brethren, all the seed of Ephraim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, pray not for this people, neither lift up cry nor prayer for them, and make not intercession to me; for I will not hear thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seest thou not what they do in the cities of Judah, and in the streets of Jerusalem?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children gather wood, and the fathers kindle the fire, and the women knead dough, to make cakes to the queen of heaven, and to pour out drink-offerings unto othergods, that they may provoke me to anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is it I whom they provoke to anger? saith Jehovah; is it not themselves, to the shame of their own face?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith the Lord Jehovah: Behold, mine anger and my fury shall be poured out upon this place; upon man, and upon beast, and upon the trees of the field, and upon the fruit of the ground; and it shall burn, and shall not be quenched.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Add your burnt-offerings to your sacrifices, and eat the flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I spoke not unto your fathers, nor commanded them concerning burnt-offerings and sacrifices, in the day that I brought them out of the land of Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I commanded them this thing, saying, Hearken unto my voice, and I will be yourGod, and ye shall be my people; and walk in all the way that I command you, that it may be well with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they hearkened not, nor inclined their ear, but walked in the counsels, in the stubbornness of their evil heart, and went backward and not forward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since the day that your fathers came forth out of the land of Egypt, unto this day, have I sent unto you all my servants the prophets, daily rising up early and sending them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but they have not hearkened unto me, nor inclined their ear; and they have hardened their neck: they have done worse than their fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt speak all these words unto them, but they will not hearken unto thee; and thou shalt call unto them, but they will not answer thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say unto them, This is the nation which hath not hearkened unto the voice of Jehovah theirGod, nor received correction; fidelity is perished, and is cut off from their mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cut off thy hair, and cast it away, and take up a lamentation on the heights; for Jehovah hath rejected and forsaken the generation of his wrath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the children of Judah have done evil in my sight, saith Jehovah; they have set their abominations in the house which is called by my name, to defile it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they have built the high places of Topheth, which is in the valley of the son of Hinnom, to burn their sons and their daughters in the fire; which I commanded not, neither did it come up into my mind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore, behold, days are coming, saith Jehovah, when it shall no more be said, Topheth, and Valley of the son of Hinnom, but the valley of slaughter; for they shall bury in Topheth, till there be no place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the carcases of this people shall be food for the fowl of the heavens, and for the beasts of the earth; and none shall scare [them] away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will cause to cease from the cities of Judah, and from the streets of Jerusalem, the voice of mirth and the voice of joy, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride; for the land shall become a waste.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time, saith Jehovah, they shall bring forth the bones of the kings of Judah, and the bones of his princes, and the bones of the priests, and the bones of the prophets, and the bones of the inhabitants of Jerusalem, out of their graves;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall spread them out to the sun and to the moon and to all the host of the heavens, which they have loved, and which they have served, and after which they have walked, and which they have sought, and which they have worshipped: they shall not be gathered, nor be buried; they shall be for dung upon the face of the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And death shall be chosen rather than life by all the residue that remain of this evil family, in all the places whither I have driven those that remain, saith Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith Jehovah: Do [men] fall, and not rise up? Doth one turn away, and not return?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why hath this people of Jerusalem slidden back with a perpetual backsliding? They hold fast deceit, they refuse to return.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I hearkened and heard: they speak not what is right; there is no man who repenteth him of his wickedness, saying, What have I done? Every one turneth to his course, like a horse rushing into the battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even a stork in the heavens knoweth her appointed times, and the turtle-dove and the swallow and the crane observe the time of their coming; but my people know not the judgment of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How do ye say, We are wise, and the law of Jehovah is with us? Behold, certainly the lying pen of the scribes hath made it falsehood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wise men are ashamed, they are dismayed and taken: behold, they have rejected Jehovah's word; and what wisdom is in them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore will I give their wives unto others, their fields to those that shall possess [them]; for every one, from the least even unto the greatest, is given to covetousness; from the prophet even unto the priest, every one dealeth falsely.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they have healed the breach of the daughter of my people lightly, saying, Peace, peace! when there is no peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are they ashamed that they have committed abomination? Nay, they are not at all ashamed, and they know not how to blush. Therefore shall they fall among them that fall: in the time of their visitation they shall stumble, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will utterly take them away, saith Jehovah: there are no grapes on the vine, nor figs on the fig-tree; and the leaf is faded: and I will give them up to those that shall pass over them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why do we sit still? Assemble yourselves, and let us enter into the fenced cities, and let us be silent there: for Jehovah ourGod hath put us to silence, and given us water of gall to drink, because we have sinned against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peace is looked for, and there is no good; a time of healing, and behold, terror.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The snorting of his horses is heard from Dan: the whole land trembleth at the sound of the neighing of his steeds, and they come, and devour the land, and all it contains, the city and those that dwell therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, I send among you serpents, vipers against which there is no charm, and they shall bite you, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My comfort in my sadness! my heart is faint in me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold the voice of the cry of the daughter of my people, from a very far country: Is not Jehovah in Zion? Is not her king in her? Why have they provoked me to anger with their graven images, with foreign vanities?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — For the breach of the daughter of my people am I crushed; I go mourning; astonishment hath taken hold of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is there no balm in Gilead? is there no physician there? Why then is there no dressing applied for the healing of the daughter of my people?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that my head were waters, and mine eye a fountain of tears, that I might weep day and night for the slain of the daughter of my people!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that I had in the wilderness a traveller's lodging-place, that I might leave my people, and go away from them! For they are all adulterers, an assembly of treacherous men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they bend their tongue, their bow of falsehood, and not for fidelity are they valiant in the land; for they proceed from evil to evil, and they know not me, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take ye heed every one of his friend, and confide not in any brother; for every brother only supplanteth, and every friend goeth about with slander.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they act deceitfully every one with his neighbour, and speak not the truth: they teach their tongue to speak falsehood, they weary themselves with perverse dealing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy habitation is in the midst of deceit; through deceit they refuse to know me, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Behold, I will melt them, and try them; for how else could I do for the daughter of my people?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their tongue is a murderous arrow; it speaketh deceit. [A man] speaketh peaceably to his neighbour with his mouth, but in his heart he layeth his ambush.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall I not visit them for these [things]? saith Jehovah; shall not my soul be avenged on such a nation as this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the mountains will I take up weeping and wailing, and for the pastures of the wilderness, a lamentation; for they are burnt up, so that none passeth through them; and the voice of the cattle is not heard. Both the fowl of the heavens and the beasts are fled; they are gone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will make Jerusalem heaps, a dwelling-place of jackals; and I will make the cities of Judah a desolation, without inhabitant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is a wise man, that he may understand this? and he to whom the mouth of Jehovah hath spoken, that he may declare it? Why is the land perished, burnt up like a wilderness, so that none passeth through?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah saith, Because they have forsaken my law which I set before them, and have not hearkened unto my voice, nor walked in it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but have walked after the stubbornness of their own heart, and after the Baals, as their fathers taught them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, Behold, I will feed this people with wormwood, and give them water of gall to drink,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and will scatter them among the nations, whom neither they nor their fathers have known; and I will send the sword after them, till I have consumed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Consider, and call for the mourning women, that they may come, and send for the skilful women, that they may come;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and let them make haste, and take up a wailing for us, that our eyes may run down with tears, and our eyelids pour forth waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a voice of wailing is heard out of Zion, How are we spoiled, sorely put to shame! For we have forsaken the land, for they have cast down our dwellings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear then the word of Jehovah, ye women, and let your ear receive the word of his mouth, and teach your daughters wailing, and each one her companion lamentation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For death is come up through our windows, is entered into our palaces, to cut off the children from the street, the young men from the broadways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak, Thus saith Jehovah: Yea, the carcases of men shall fall as dung upon the open field, and as the handful after the reaper, and there shall be none to gather.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Let not the wise glory in his wisdom, neither let the mighty glory in his might; let not the rich glory in his riches:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but let him that glorieth glory in this, that he understandeth and knoweth me, that I [am] Jehovah, who exercise loving-kindness, judgment, and righteousness in the earth; for in these things I delight, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, days are coming, saith Jehovah, when I will visit all [them that are] circumcised with the uncircumcised;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Egypt, and Judah, and Edom, and the children of Ammon, and Moab, and all that have the corners [of their beard] cut off, that dwell in the wilderness: for all the nations are uncircumcised, and all the house of Israel are uncircumcised in heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear the word that Jehovah speaketh unto you, house of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Learn not the way of the nations, and be not dismayed at the signs of the heavens; for the nations are dismayed at them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the statutes of the peoples are vanity; for [it is] a tree cut out of the forest, worked with a chisel by the hands of the artizan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they deck it with silver and with gold; they fasten it with nails and with hammers, that it move not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are as a palm-column of turned work, and they speak not; they are carried, for they cannot go. Be not afraid of them; for they cannot do evil, neither also is it in them to do good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is none like unto thee, Jehovah; thou art great, and thy name is great in might.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who would not fear thee, O King of nations? For to thee doth it appertain; for among all the wise men of the nations, and in all their kingdoms, there is none like unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they are one and all senseless and foolish; the teaching of vanities is a stock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Silver spread into plates is brought from Tarshish, and gold from Uphaz, the work of the artizan and of the hands of the founder; blue and purple is their clothing: they are all the work of skilful [men].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah Elohim is truth; he is the livingGod, and the King of eternity. At his wrath the earth trembleth, and the nations cannot abide his indignation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus shall ye say unto them: Thegods that have not made the heavens and the earth, these shall perish from the earth, and from under the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heavens by his understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When he uttereth his voice, there is a tumult of waters in the heavens, and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the end of the earth; he maketh lightnings for the rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasuries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every man is become brutish, bereft of knowledge; every founder is put to shame by the graven image, for his molten image is falsehood, and there is no breath in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are vanity, a work of delusion: in the time of their visitation they shall perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The portion of Jacob is not like them; for it is he that hath formed all [things], and Israel is the rod of his inheritance: Jehovah of hosts is his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gather up thy baggage out of the land, O inhabitress of the fortress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will this time sling out the inhabitants of the land, and will distress them, that they may be found.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe is me, for my wound! My stroke is hard to heal, and I had said, Yea, this is [my] grief, and I will bear it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My tent is despoiled, and all my cords are broken; my children are gone forth from me, and they are not; there is none to stretch forth my tent any more, and to set up my curtains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the shepherds are become brutish, and have not sought Jehovah; therefore have they not acted wisely, and all their flock is scattered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of a rumour! Behold, it cometh, and a great commotion out of the north country, to make the cities of Judah a desolation, a dwelling-place of jackals.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I know, Jehovah, that the way of man is not his own; it is not in a man that walketh to direct his steps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, correct me, but with judgment; not in thine anger, lest thou bring me to nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pour out thy fury upon the nations that know thee not, and upon the families that call not on thy name; for they have eaten up Jacob, yea, they have eaten him up and consumed him, and have laid waste his dwelling-place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear ye the words of this covenant; and speak ye unto the men of Judah, and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith Jehovah, theGod of Israel: Cursed be the man that obeyeth not the words of this covenant

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which I commanded your fathers in the day that I brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, from the iron furnace, saying, Hearken unto my voice and do them, according to all that I command you; so shall ye be my people, and I will be yourGod:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that I may perform the oath that I have sworn unto your fathers, to give them a land flowing with milk and honey, as it is this day. And I answered and said, Amen, Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, Proclaim all these words in the cities of Judah, and in the streets of Jerusalem, saying: Hear the words of this covenant, and do them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I earnestly protested unto your fathers, in the day that I brought them up out of the land of Egypt, unto this day, rising early and protesting, saying, Hearken unto my voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they have not hearkened, nor inclined their ear, but have walked every one in the stubbornness of their evil heart; and I have brought upon them all the words of this covenant, which I commanded [them] to do, and they have not done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, A conspiracy is found among the men of Judah, and among the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are turned back to the iniquities of their forefathers, who refused to hear my words; and they have gone after othergods to serve them. The house of Israel and the house of Judah have broken my covenant which I made with their fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will bring evil upon them, from which they shall not be able to escape; and they will cry unto me, and I will not hearken unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall the cities of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem go and cry unto thegods unto whom they have burned incense; but they shall not save them at all in the time of their trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For [as] the number of thy cities, are thygods, O Judah; and [as] the number of the streets of Jerusalem, have ye set up altars to the Shame, altars to burn incense unto Baal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, pray not for this people, neither lift up cry nor prayer for them; for I will not hear in the time that they cry unto me for their trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What hath my beloved to do in my house, seeing that the more part practise their evil devices, and the holy flesh is passed from thee? When thou doest evil, then thou rejoicest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah had called thy name, A green olive-tree, fair, of goodly fruit: with the noise of a great tumult he hath kindled fire upon it, and its branches are broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah of hosts, that planted thee, hath pronounced evil against thee, for the evil of the house of Israel and of the house of Judah, which they have done for themselves, to provoke me to anger in burning incense unto Baal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah hath given me knowledge, and I know [it]; then thou shewedst me their doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I was like a tame lamb [that] is led to the slaughter; and I knew not that they devised devices against me, [saying,] Let us destroy the tree with the fruit thereof, and let us cut him off from the land of the living, that his name may be no more remembered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, Jehovah of hosts, who judgest righteously, who triest the reins and the heart, let me see thy vengeance on them: for unto thee have I revealed my cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah concerning the men of Anathoth, that seek thy life, saying, Prophesy not in the name of Jehovah, that thou die not by our hand,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Behold, I punish them: the young men shall die by the sword; their sons and their daughters shall die by famine;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there shall be no remnant of them; for I will bring evil upon the men of Anathoth, in the year of their visitation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Righteous art thou, Jehovah, when I plead with thee; yet will I speak with thee of [thy] judgments. Wherefore doth the way of the wicked prosper? [wherefore] are all they at ease that deal very treacherously?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast planted them, they also have taken root: they advance, yea, they bring forth fruit: thou art near in their mouth, but far from their reins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, Jehovah, knowest me; thou hast seen me, and proved my heart toward thee. Drag them out like sheep for the slaughter, and set them apart for the day of slaughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long shall the land mourn, and the herbs of all the fields wither? Because of the wickedness of them that dwell therein, the beasts and the birds perish; for they say, He will not see our end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou hast run with footmen, and they have wearied thee, how wilt thou then contend with horses? And if in a land of peace thou thinkest thyself in security, how wilt thou then do in the swelling of the Jordan?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For even thy brethren, and the house of thy father, even they have dealt treacherously with thee, even they have cried aloud after thee. Believe them not, though they speak good [words] unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have forsaken my house, I have cast off my heritage, I have given the beloved of my soul into the hand of her enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heritage is become unto me as a lion in the forest; it hath raised its voice against me: therefore have I hated it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heritage is unto me [as] a speckled bird of prey; the birds of prey round about are against her. Go, assemble all the beasts of the field, bring them to devour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many shepherds have destroyed my vineyard, they have trodden my portion under foot, they have made my pleasant portion a desolate wilderness:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they have made it a desolation; desolate, it mourneth unto me: the whole land is made desolate, for no man layeth it to heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Spoilers are come upon all heights in the wilderness; for the sword of Jehovah devoureth from one end of the land even to the [other] end of the land: no flesh hath peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have sown wheat, and they reap thorns; they have put themselves to pain, [and] do not profit. Be ye therefore ashamed of your revenues, because of the fierce anger of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah against all mine evil neighbours, that touch the inheritance which I have caused my people Israel to inherit: Behold, I will pluck them up out of their land, and pluck out the house of Judah from among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, after I have plucked them up, I will return, and have compassion on them, and will bring them back, each one to his inheritance, and each one to his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, if they will diligently learn the ways of my people, to swear by my name, [As] Jehovah liveth — even as they taught my people to swear by Baal — they shall be built up in the midst of my people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if they will not obey, I will utterly pluck up and destroy that nation, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus said Jehovah unto me: Go and buy thee a linen girdle, and put it upon thy loins; but dip it not in water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I bought a girdle according to the word of Jehovah, and put it upon my loins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto me the second time, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take the girdle that thou hast bought, which is upon thy loins, and arise, go to the Euphrates, and hide it there in a hole of the rock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So I went and hid it by the Euphrates, as Jehovah had commanded me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of many days, that Jehovah said unto me, Arise, go to the Euphrates, and take the girdle from thence which I commanded thee to hide there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I went to the Euphrates, and digged, and took the girdle from the place where I had hid it; and behold, the girdle was spoiled, it was good for nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: After this manner will I spoil the pride of Judah, and the great pride of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This evil people, who refuse to hear my words, who walk in the stubbornness of their heart, and go after othergods, to serve them and to worship them, shall even be as this girdle which is good for nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as a girdle cleaveth to the loins of a man, so have I caused to cleave unto me the whole house of Israel and the whole house of Judah, saith Jehovah; that they might be unto me for a people, and for a name, and for a praise, and for a glory: but they would not hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt speak unto them this word: Thus saith Jehovah, theGod of Israel, Every skin shall be filled with wine. And they will say unto thee, Do we not very well know that every skin shall be filled with wine?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will fill all the inhabitants of this land, and the kings that sit for David upon his throne, and the priests and the prophets, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, with drunkenness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will dash them one against another, both the fathers and the sons together, saith Jehovah; I will not pity, nor spare, nor have mercy so as not to destroy them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear ye, and give ear, be not lifted up; for Jehovah hath spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give glory to Jehovah yourGod, before he cause darkness, and before your feet stumble upon the mountains of twilight; and ye shall look for light, but he will turn it into the shadow of death, and make [it] gross darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if ye will not hear it, my soul shall weep in secret places for [your] pride; and mine eye shall weep sore, and run down with tears, because Jehovah's flock is gone into captivity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Say unto the king and to the queen: Humble yourselves, sit down low; for from your heads shall come down the crown of your magnificence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The cities of the south are shut up, and there is none to open [them]; all Judah is carried away captive: it is wholly carried away captive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift up your eyes, and behold them that come from the north. Where is the flock that was given thee, thy beautiful flock?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What wilt thou say when he shall visit thee, since thou thyself hast trained them to be princes in chief over thee? Shall not sorrows take thee, as a woman in travail?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thou say in thy heart, Wherefore come these things upon me? For the greatness of thine iniquity are thy skirts uncovered, [and] thy heels have suffered violence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Can an Ethiopian change his skin, or a leopard his spots? [Then] may ye also do good, who are accustomed to do evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will scatter them as stubble that passeth away by the wind of the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This shall be thy lot, thy measured portion from me, saith Jehovah; because thou hast forgotten me, and confided in falsehood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore will I also turn thy skirts over thy face, and thy shame shall be seen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine adulteries, and thy neighings, the lewdness of thy fornication, on the hills, in the fields, — thine abominations, have I seen. Woe unto thee, Jerusalem! Wilt thou not be made clean? after how long a time yet?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word of Jehovah that came to Jeremiah concerning the drought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judah mourneth, and the gates thereof languish, they are black unto the ground; and the cry of Jerusalem goeth up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their nobles send their little ones for water: they come to the pits, they find no water; they return with their vessels empty; they are ashamed, they are confounded, and have covered their heads.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because the ground is chapt, for there hath been no rain on the earth, the ploughmen are ashamed, they cover their heads.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the hind also calveth in the field, and forsaketh [its young], because there is no grass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the wild asses stand on the heights, they snuff up the wind like jackals; their eyes fail, because there is no herbage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, though our iniquities testify against us, do thou act for thy name's sake; for our backslidings are many — we have sinned against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hope of Israel, its Saviour in the time of trouble, why wilt thou be as a stranger in the land, and as a traveller that turneth aside to stay a night?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why wilt thou be as a man astonished, as a mighty man that cannot save? Yet thou, Jehovah, art in the midst of us, and we are called by thy name: leave us not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah to this people: Even so have they loved to wander, they have not refrained their feet; and Jehovah hath no delight in them: now will he remember their iniquity, and visit their sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, Pray not for this people for their good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When they fast, I will not hear their cry; and when they offer up burnt-offering and oblation, I will not accept them: for I will consume them by sword, and by famine, and by pestilence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, Alas, Lord Jehovah! Behold, the prophets say unto them, Ye shall not see the sword, neither shall ye have famine; for I will give you assured peace in this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, The prophets prophesy falsehood in my name; I have not sent them, neither have I commanded them, nor spoken unto them: they prophesy unto you a false vision, and divination, and a thing of nought, and the deceit of their heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah concerning the prophets that prophesy in my name, and I sent them not, and who say, Sword and famine shall not be in this land: By sword and by famine shall those prophets be consumed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the people to whom they prophesy shall be cast out in the streets of Jerusalem, because of the famine and the sword; and there shall be none to bury them, them, their wives, and their sons, and their daughters; and I will pour their wickedness upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say this word unto them: Let mine eyes run down with tears, night and day, and not cease; for the virgin daughter of my people is broken with a great breach, with a very grievous blow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I go forth into the field, behold the slain with the sword! and if I enter into the city, behold them that pine away with famine! For both prophet and priest shall go about into a land that they know not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Hast thou then utterly rejected Judah? Doth thy soul loathe Zion? Why hast thou smitten us, and there is no healing for us? Peace is looked for, and there is no good, — and a time of healing, and behold terror!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, we acknowledge our wickedness, the iniquity of our fathers; for we have sinned against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thy name's sake, do not spurn [us], do not disgrace the throne of thy glory: remember, break not thy covenant with us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are there any among the vanities of the nations that can cause rain? or can the heavens give showers? Art not thou HE, Jehovah, ourGod? And we wait upon thee; for thou hast made all these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, Though Moses and Samuel stood before me, my soul [would] not [turn] toward this people. Send [them] out of my sight, and let them go forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, if they say unto thee, Whither shall we go forth? then thou shalt tell them, Thus saith Jehovah: Such as are for death, to death; and such as are for the sword, to the sword; and such as are for the famine, to the famine; and such as are for captivity, to captivity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I will visit them with four kinds [of punishments], saith Jehovah: the sword to slay, and dogs to tear, and the fowl of the heavens, and the beasts of the earth, to devour and to destroy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give them over to be driven hither and thither amongst all the kingdoms of the earth, because of Manasseh the son of Hezekiah, king of Judah, for that which he did in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For who shall have pity upon thee, Jerusalem? and who shall bemoan thee? and who shall turn aside to ask after thy welfare?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast cast me off, saith Jehovah, thou art gone backward; and I have stretched out my hand against thee, and will destroy thee: I am become weary of repenting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will fan them with a fan in the gates of the land; I will bereave of children [and] destroy my people: they have not returned from their ways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their widows are increased to me more than the sand of the seas; I have brought upon them, against the mother of the young men, a spoiler at noonday; I have caused anguish and terror to fall upon her suddenly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She that hath borne seven languisheth, she hath given up the ghost; her sun is gone down while it is yet day; she is put to shame and confounded. And the residue of them will I give up to the sword before their enemies, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe is me, my mother, that thou hast borne me a man of strife and a man of contention to the whole land! I have not lent on usury, nor have they lent to me on usury; [yet] every one of them doth curse me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah said, Verily I will set thee free for [thy] good; verily I will cause the enemy to meet thee kindly in the time of evil and in the time of affliction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will iron break? iron from the north? and bronze?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy substance and thy treasures will I give to the spoil without price, and that for all thy sins, and in all thy borders;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will make [them] to pass with thine enemies into a land that thou knowest not: for a fire is kindled in mine anger; it shall burn upon you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, thou knowest: remember me, and visit me, and avenge me of my persecutors; in thy long-suffering take me not away: know that for thy sake I bear reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy words were found, and I did eat them, and thy words were unto me the joy and rejoicing of my heart; for I am called by thy name, O Jehovah,God of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I sat not in the assembly of the mockers, nor exulted: I sat alone because of thy hand; for thou hast filled me with indignation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why is my pain perpetual, and my wound incurable? It refuseth to be healed. Wilt thou be altogether unto me as a treacherous [spring], [as] waters that fail?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah: If thou return, then will I bring thee again, thou shalt stand before me; and if thou take forth the precious from the vile, thou shalt be as my mouth. Let them return unto thee; but return not thou unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will make thee unto this people a strong brazen wall; and they shall fight against thee, but they shall not prevail against thee: for I am with thee, to save thee and to deliver thee, saith Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: yea, I will deliver thee out of the hand of the wicked, and I will redeem thee out of the hand of the terrible.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
