translations.text: Thou shalt not take thee a wife, and thou shalt not have sons nor daughters in this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah concerning the sons and concerning the daughters that are born in this place, and concerning their mothers that bear them, and concerning their fathers that beget them in this land:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall die of painful deaths; they shall not be lamented, neither shall they be buried; they shall be as dung upon the face of the ground, and they shall be consumed by the sword, and by famine, and their carcases shall be food for the fowl of the heavens and for the beasts of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: Enter not into the house of wailing, neither go to lament or bemoan them; for I have taken away my peace from this people, saith Jehovah, the loving-kindness and the tender mercies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Both great and small shall die in this land: they shall not be buried; and none shall lament for them, or cut themselves, nor make themselves bald for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nor shall they break [bread] for them in mourning, to comfort them for the dead; neither shall they give them the cup of consolations to drink for their father or for their mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt not go into the house of feasting, to sit with them, to eat and to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Behold, I will cause to cease out of this place, before your eyes, and in your days, the voice of mirth and the voice of joy, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when thou shalt declare unto this people all these words, and they shall say unto thee, Wherefore hath Jehovah pronounced all this great evil against us? and what is our iniquity? and what is our sin which we have committed against Jehovah ourGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then shalt thou say unto them, Because your fathers have forsaken me, saith Jehovah, and have walked after othergods, and have served them, and have worshipped them, and have forsaken me, and have not kept my law;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and you, ye have done still worse than your fathers; and there ye are walking every one after the stubbornness of his evil heart, not to hearken unto me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will cast you forth out of this land, into a land that ye know not, ye nor your fathers; and there shall ye serve othergods day and night: because I will shew you no favour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore, behold, days are coming, saith Jehovah, that it shall no more be said, [As] Jehovah liveth, who brought up the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but, [As] Jehovah liveth, who brought up the children of Israel from the land of the north, and from all the lands whither he had driven them. For I will bring them again into their land, which I gave unto their fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will send for many fishers, saith Jehovah, and they shall fish them; and afterwards will I send for many hunters, and they shall hunt them, from every mountain, and from every hill, and out of the holes of the rocks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For mine eyes are upon all their ways; they are not concealed from my face, neither is their iniquity hidden from before mine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But first I will recompense their iniquity and their sin double, because they have profaned my land with the carcases of their detestable things, and with their abominations have they filled mine inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, my strength and my fortress, and my refuge in the day of distress, unto thee shall the nations come from the ends of the earth, and they shall say, Surely our fathers have inherited falsehood [and] vanity; and in these things there is no profit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall a man makegods unto himself, and they are no-gods?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore, behold, I will this once cause them to know, I will cause them to know my hand and my might; and they shall know that my name is Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sin of Judah is written with a style of iron, with the point of a diamond, engraven upon the tablet of their heart, and upon the horns of your altars;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whilst their children remember their altars and their Asherahs, by the green trees, upon the high hills.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My mountain in the field, thy substance, all thy treasures will I give for a spoil, — thy high places, because of sin throughout thy borders.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of thyself thou shalt let go thine inheritance which I gave thee; and I will cause thee to serve thine enemies in a land that thou knowest not; for ye have kindled a fire in mine anger, — it shall burn for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Cursed is the man that confideth in man, and maketh flesh his arm, and whose heart departeth from Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall be like the heath in the desert, and shall not see when good cometh; but he shall inhabit the parched places in the wilderness, a salt land and not inhabited.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man that confideth in Jehovah, and whose confidence Jehovah is.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he shall be like a tree planted by the waters, and that spreadeth out its roots by the stream, and he shall not see when heat cometh, but his leaf shall be green; and in the year of drought he shall not be careful, neither shall he cease to yield fruit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heart is deceitful above all things, and incurable; who can know it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I Jehovah search the heart, I try the reins, even to give each one according to his ways, according to the fruit of his doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] the partridge sitteth on [eggs] it hath not laid, [so] is he that getteth riches and not by right: in the midst of his days shall he leave them, and at his end shall be a fool.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A throne of glory, [set] on high from the beginning, is the place of our sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hope of Israel, Jehovah! all that forsake thee shall be ashamed. They that depart from me shall be written in the earth; because they have forsaken Jehovah, the fountain of living waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Heal me, Jehovah, and I shall be healed; save me, and I shall be saved: for thou art my praise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, these say unto me, Where is the word of Jehovah? let it then come!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, I have not hastened from being a shepherd in following thee, neither have I desired the fatal day, thou knowest: that which came out of my lips was before thy face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not a terror unto me: thou art my refuge in the day of evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be ashamed that persecute me, but let not me be ashamed; let them be dismayed, but let not me be dismayed; bring upon them the day of evil, and break them with a double breaking.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus hath Jehovah said unto me: Go and stand in the gate of the children of the people, by which the kings of Judah come in, and by which they go out, and in all the gates of Jerusalem;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and say unto them, Hear the word of Jehovah, ye kings of Judah, and all Judah, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, that enter in by these gates;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thus saith Jehovah: Take heed to your souls, and bear no burden on the sabbath day, and bring nothing in through the gates of Jerusalem;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and carry forth no burden out of your houses on the sabbath day, neither do any work; but hallow ye the sabbath day, as I commanded your fathers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but they hearkened not, neither inclined their ear, but hardened their neck, that they might not hear nor receive instruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, if ye diligently hearken unto me, saith Jehovah, to bring in no burden through the gates of this city on the sabbath day, and to hallow the sabbath day, to do no work therein;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then shall there enter in, through the gates of this city, kings and princes sitting upon the throne of David, riding in chariots and on horses, they and their princes, the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem: and this city shall be inhabited for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall come from the cities of Judah, and from the places around Jerusalem, and from the land of Benjamin, and from the lowland, and from the hill-country, and from the south, bringing burnt-offerings, and sacrifices, and oblations, and incense, and bringing thanksgiving unto the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye will not hearken unto me, to hallow the sabbath day and not to bear a burden and enter in through the gates of Jerusalem on the sabbath day, then will I kindle a fire in the gates thereof, and it shall devour the palaces of Jerusalem, and it shall not be quenched.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise and go down to the potter's house, and there I will cause thee to hear my words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I went down to the potter's house; and behold, he wrought a work on the wheels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the vessel that he made was marred, as clay, in the hand of the potter; and he made it again another vessel, as seemed good to the potter to make.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: House of Israel, cannot I do with you as this potter? saith Jehovah. Behold, as the clay in the potter's hand, so are ye in my hand, house of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the moment that I speak concerning a nation and concerning a kingdom, to pluck up, and to break down, and to destroy,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if that nation, concerning which I have spoken, turn from their evil, then I will repent of the evil that I thought to do unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And at the moment that I speak concerning a nation and concerning a kingdom, to build and to plant,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if it do evil in my sight, that it hearken not unto my voice, then I will repent of the good wherewith I said I would benefit them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, speak to the men of Judah and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, saying, Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I prepare evil against you, and devise a device against you: turn ye then every one from his evil way, and amend your ways and your doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they say, There is no hope; for we will walk after our own devices, and we will each one do [according to] the stubbornness of his evil heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Ask ye now among the nations, Who hath heard such things? The virgin of Israel hath done a very horrible thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall the snow of Lebanon cease from the rock of the field? Shall the cool flowing waters coming from afar be dried up?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my people hath forgotten me: they burn incense to vanity; and they have caused them to stumble in their ways, the ancient paths, to walk in by-paths of a way not cast up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to make their land an astonishment, a perpetual hissing: every one that passeth by shall be astonished, and shake his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As with an east wind will I scatter them before the enemy; I will shew them the back, and not the face, in the day of their calamity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Come, and let us devise devices against Jeremiah; for law shall not perish from the priest, nor counsel from the wise, nor word from the prophet. Come and let us smite him with the tongue, and let us not give heed to any of his words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, give heed to me, and listen to the voice of those that contend with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall evil be recompensed for good? For they have digged a pit for my soul. Remember how I stood before thee to speak good for them, to turn away thy wrath from them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore give up their children to the famine, and deliver them over to the power of the sword; and let their wives be bereaved of children and be widows; and let their men be swept off by death, their young men be smitten by the sword in battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let a cry be heard from their houses, when thou shalt bring a troop suddenly upon them; for they have digged a pit to take me, and have hidden snares for my feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Jehovah, knowest all their counsel against me to slay me. Forgive not their iniquity, neither blot out their sin from thy sight, but let them be overthrown before thee: deal with them in the time of thine anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Go and buy a potter's earthen flagon, and [take] of the elders of the people, and of the elders of the priests;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and go forth unto the valley of the son of Hinnom, which is by the entry of the pottery-gate, and proclaim there the words that I shall tell thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and say, Hear the word of Jehovah, O kings of Judah, and inhabitants of Jerusalem. Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Behold, I will bring evil upon this place, the which whosoever heareth, his ears shall tingle;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because they have forsaken me, and have estranged this place [from me], and have burned incense in it unto othergods, whom neither they nor their fathers have known, nor the kings of Judah; and have filled this place with the blood of innocents;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they have built the high places of Baal, to burn their sons in the fire as burnt-offerings unto Baal, which I commanded not, nor spake it, neither came it up into my mind:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore behold, days come, saith Jehovah, that this place shall no more be called Topheth, nor Valley of the son of Hinnom, but the valley of slaughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will make void the counsel of Judah and Jerusalem in this place; and I will cause them to fall by the sword before their enemies, and by the hand of them that seek their life, and their carcases will I give as food to the fowl of the heavens and to the beasts of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will make this city an astonishment and a hissing; every one that passeth by shall be astonished and hiss because of all the plagues thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will cause them to eat the flesh of their sons and the flesh of their daughters, and they shall eat everyone the flesh of his friend, in the siege and in the straitness wherewith their enemies, and they that seek their lives, shall straiten them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt break the flagon in the sight of the men that go with thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shalt say unto them, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Even so will I break this people and this city, as one breaketh a potter's vessel, that cannot be made whole again. And they shall bury in Topheth, till there be no place to bury.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus will I do unto this place, saith Jehovah, and to the inhabitants thereof, and make this city as Topheth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the houses of Jerusalem and the houses of the kings of Judah shall be as the place of Topheth, defiled, all the houses upon whose roofs they have burned incense unto all the host of the heavens, and have poured out drink-offerings unto othergods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah came from Topheth, whither Jehovah had sent him to prophesy; and he stood in the court of Jehovah's house, and said to all the people,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Behold, I will bring upon this city and upon all her cities all the evil that I have spoken against it; for they have hardened their necks, not to hear my words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pashur the son of Immer, the priest — and he was chief officer in the house of Jehovah — heard Jeremiah prophesy these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pashur smote Jeremiah the prophet, and put him in the stocks that were in the upper gate of Benjamin, which was in the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the next day, that Pashur brought forth Jeremiah out of the stocks; and Jeremiah said unto him, Jehovah hath not called thy name Pashur, but Magor-missabib.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I make thee a terror to thyself, and to all thy friends; and they shall fall by the sword of their enemies, and thine eyes shall see [it]; and I will give all Judah into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall carry them captive into Babylon, and shall smite them with the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, I will give all the wealth of this city, and all its gains, and all its precious things, and all the treasures of the kings of Judah, will I give into the hand of their enemies; and they shall make them a prey, and take them, and carry them to Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Pashur, and all that dwell in thy house shall go into captivity; and thou shalt come to Babylon, and there thou shalt die, and there thou shalt be buried, thou and all thy friends to whom thou hast prophesied falsehood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, thou hast enticed me, and I was enticed; thou hast laid hold of me, and hast prevailed; I am become a derision the whole day: every one mocketh me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as oft as I speak, I cry out; I proclaim violence and spoil; for the word of Jehovah is become unto me a reproach and a derision all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, I will not make mention of him, nor speak any more in his name: but it was in my heart as a burning fire shut up in my bones; and I became wearied with holding in, and I could not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have heard the defaming of many, terror on every side: Report, and we will report it. All my familiars are watching for my stumbling: Peradventure he will be enticed, and we shall prevail against him; and we shall take our revenge on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah is with me as a mighty terrible one; therefore my persecutors shall stumble and shall not prevail; they shall be greatly ashamed, for they have not prospered: it shall be an everlasting confusion that shall not be forgotten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Jehovah of hosts, who triest the righteous, who seest the reins and the heart, let me see thy vengeance on them; for unto thee have I revealed my cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing ye unto Jehovah, praise Jehovah, for he hath delivered the soul of the needy from the hand of evildoers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cursed be the day wherein I was born; let not the day wherein my mother bore me be blessed!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cursed be the man who brought tidings to my father, saying, A man child is born unto thee; making him very glad!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let that man be as the cities which Jehovah overthrew, and repented not; and let him hear a cry in the morning, and a shouting at noonday,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because he slew me not from the womb. Or would that my mother had been my grave, and her womb always great [with me]!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore came I forth from the womb to see labour and sorrow, that my days should be consumed in shame?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came unto Jeremiah from Jehovah, when king Zedekiah sent unto him Pashur the son of Malchijah, and Zephaniah the son of Maaseiah, the priest, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Inquire, I pray thee, of Jehovah for us; for Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon maketh war against us; if so be that Jehovah will deal with us according to all his marvellous works, that he may go up from us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said unto them, Thus shall ye say to Zedekiah:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel: Behold, I will turn back the weapons of war that are in your hands, with which ye fight against the king of Babylon, and against the Chaldeans who besiege you, outside the walls, and I will assemble them into the midst of this city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I myself will fight against you with a stretched-out hand, and with a strong arm, and in anger, and in fury, and in great wrath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will smite the inhabitants of this city, both man and beast: they shall die of a great pestilence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards, saith Jehovah, I will give Zedekiah king of Judah, and his servants, and the people, and such as are left in this city from the pestilence, from the sword, and from the famine, into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon, and into the hand of their enemies, and into the hand of those that seek their life, and he shall smite them with the edge of the sword: he shall not spare them, neither have pity, nor have mercy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And unto this people thou shalt say, Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I set before you the way of life, and the way of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that abideth in this city shall die by the sword, and by the famine, and by the pestilence; but he that goeth out, and deserteth to the Chaldeans that besiege you, he shall live, and his life shall be unto him for a prey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have set my face against this city for evil, and not for good, saith Jehovah: it shall be given into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall burn it with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And touching the house of the king of Judah, hear ye the word of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: House of David, thus saith Jehovah: Judge with justice in the morning, and deliver him that is spoiled out of the hand of the oppressor, lest my fury go forth like fire and burn, and there be none to quench it, because of the evil of your doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I am against thee, inhabitress of the valley, the rock of the plain, saith Jehovah; ye that say, Who shall come down against us, or who shall enter into our dwellings?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will visit you according to the fruit of your doings, saith Jehovah; and I will kindle a fire in her forest, and it shall devour all that is round about her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Go down to the house of the king of Judah, and speak there this word,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and say, Hear the word of Jehovah, O king of Judah, that sittest upon the throne of David, thou, and thy servants, and thy people who enter in through these gates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Execute judgment and righteousness, and deliver the spoiled out of the hand of the oppressor; and do no wrong, do no violence to the stranger, the fatherless, or the widow, and shed not innocent blood in this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if ye do this thing indeed, then shall there enter in through the gates of this house kings sitting in the place of David upon his throne, riding in chariots and on horses, — he, and his servants, and his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye will not hear these words, I have sworn by myself, saith Jehovah, that this house shall become a waste.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah concerning the house of the king of Judah: Thou art a Gilead unto me, the summit of Lebanon: verily I will make thee a wilderness, cities not inhabited.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will prepare destroyers against thee, every one with his weapons; and they shall cut down the choice of thy cedars, and cast [them] into the fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And many nations shall pass by this city, and they shall say every man to his neighbour, Wherefore hath Jehovah done thus unto this great city?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall say, Because they have forsaken the covenant of Jehovah theirGod, and worshipped othergods, and served them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Weep not for the dead, neither bemoan him; [but] weep sore for him that goeth away, for he shall return no more, nor see his native country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah concerning Shallum the son of Josiah, the king of Judah, who reigned instead of Josiah his father, who went forth out of this place: He shall not return thither any more;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he shall die in the place whither they have led him captive, and shall see this land no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe unto him that buildeth his house by unrighteousness, and his upper chambers by injustice; that taketh his neighbour's service without wages, and giveth him not his earning;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that saith, I will build me a wide house, and spacious upper chambers; and he cutteth out for himself windows; and it is wainscoted with cedar, and painted with vermilion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shalt thou reign, because thou viest with the cedar? Did not thy father eat and drink, and do judgment and justice? Then it was well with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He judged the cause of the poor and needy; then it was well. Was not this to know me? saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thine eyes and thy heart are only on thine extortion, and on the blood of the innocent, to shed it, and on oppression and on violence, to do it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah concerning Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah: They shall not lament for him, Ah, my brother! or, Ah, sister! They shall not lament for him, Ah, lord! or Ah, his glory!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall be buried with the burial of an ass, dragged along and cast forth beyond the gates of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go up to Lebanon, and cry; and give forth thy voice in Bashan, and cry from [the heights of] Abarim: for all thy lovers are destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I spoke unto thee in thy prosperity; [but] thou saidst, I will not hear. This hath been thy way from thy youth, that thou hearkenedst not unto my voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wind shall feed on all thy shepherds, and thy lovers shall go into captivity; surely, then shalt thou be ashamed and confounded for all thy wickedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou inhabitress of Lebanon, that makest thy nest in the cedars, how pitiful shalt thou be when pangs come upon thee, pain as of a woman in travail!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] I live, saith Jehovah, though Coniah the son of Jehoiakim, the king of Judah, were a signet upon my right hand, yet will I pluck thee thence;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will give thee into the hand of them that seek thy life, and into the hand of them before whom thou art afraid, even into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of the Chaldeans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will cast thee out, and thy mother that bare thee, into another country, where ye were not born; and there shall ye die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And into the land whereunto they lift up their souls to return, thither shall they not return.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is this man Coniah a despised broken vase? a vessel wherein is no delight? Wherefore are they thrown out, he and his seed, and are cast into a land which they know not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O earth, earth, earth, hear the word of Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Write this man childless, a man that shall not prosper in his days; for no man of his seed shall prosper, sitting upon the throne of David, and ruling any more in Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe unto the shepherds that destroy and scatter the sheep of my pasture! saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel concerning the shepherds that feed my people: Ye have scattered my flock, and driven them away, and have not visited them: behold, I will visit upon you the evil of your doings, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will gather the remnant of my flock out of all countries whither I have driven them, and will bring them again to their pastures; and they shall be fruitful and shall multiply.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will raise up shepherds over them, who shall feed them; and they shall fear no more, nor be dismayed, neither shall any be missing, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the days come, saith Jehovah, when I will raise unto David a righteous Branch, who shall reign as king, and act wisely, and shall execute judgment and righteousness in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In his days Judah shall be saved, and Israel shall dwell in safety; and this is his name whereby he shall be called, Jehovah our Righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore behold, days are coming, saith Jehovah, that they shall no more say, [As] Jehovah liveth, who brought up the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but, [As] Jehovah liveth, who brought up and who led back the seed of the house of Israel out of the north country, and from all countries whither I had driven them. And they shall dwell in their own land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Concerning the prophets: My heart within me is broken; all my bones shake; I am like a drunken man, and like a man whom wine hath overcome; because of Jehovah, and because of the words of his holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the land is full of adulterers; for because of execration the land mourneth. The pastures of the wilderness are dried up; for their course is evil, and their force is not right.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For both prophet and priest are profane: even in my house have I found their wickedness, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore their way shall be unto them as slippery places in the darkness; they shall be driven on, and fall therein: for I will bring evil upon them in the year of their visitation, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have seen folly in the prophets of Samaria: they prophesied by Baal, and caused my people Israel to err.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the prophets of Jerusalem have I seen a horrible thing: they commit adultery, and walk in falsehood, and strengthen the hands of evildoers, so that none doth return from his wickedness. They are all become unto me as Sodom, and the inhabitants thereof as Gomorrah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts concerning the prophets: Behold, I will feed them with wormwood, and make them drink water of gall; for from the prophets of Jerusalem is profaneness gone forth into all the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Hearken not unto the words of the prophets that prophesy unto you: they lead you to vanity; they speak a vision of their own heart, not out of the mouth of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say constantly unto them that despise me, Jehovah hath said, Ye shall have peace. And they say unto every one that walketh in the stubbornness of his heart, No evil shall come upon you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For who hath stood in the council of Jehovah, so that he hath perceived and heard his word? who hath hearkened to his word and listened?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, a tempest of Jehovah, fury is gone forth, yea, a whirling storm: it shall whirl down upon the head of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The anger of Jehovah shall not return, until he have executed, and until he have performed the purposes of his heart: at the end of the days ye shall understand it clearly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I did not send the prophets, yet they ran; I have not spoken to them, yet they prophesied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if they had stood in my council, and had caused my people to hear my words, then would they have turned them from their evil way and from the wickedness of their doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Am I aGod at hand, saith Jehovah, and not aGod afar off?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Can any hide himself in secret places, that I shall not see him? saith Jehovah. Do not I fill the heavens and the earth? saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have heard what the prophets say, who prophesy falsehood in my name, saying, I have dreamed, I have dreamed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long shall [this] be in the heart of the prophets who prophesy falsehood, and who are prophets of the deceit of their own heart?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who think to cause my people to forget my name by their dreams which they tell every man to his neighbour: as their fathers have forgotten my name for Baal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The prophet that hath a dream, let him tell the dream; and he that hath my word, let him speak my word faithfully. What is the chaff to the wheat? saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not my word like a fire, saith Jehovah; and like a hammer [that] breaketh the rock in pieces?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore, behold, I am against the prophets, saith Jehovah, that steal my words every one from his neighbour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I am against the prophets, saith Jehovah, that use their tongues, and say, He hath said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I am against them that prophesy false dreams, saith Jehovah, and that tell them, and cause my people to err by their lies and by their boasting; and I have not sent them, nor commanded them; and they profit not this people at all, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when this people, or a prophet, or a priest, ask thee, saying, What is the burden of Jehovah? thou shalt then say unto them, What burden? I will even cast you off, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as for the prophet, and the priest, and the people, that shall say, The burden of Jehovah, I will even punish that man and his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus shall ye say every one to his neighbour, and every one to his brother: What hath Jehovah answered? and, What hath Jehovah spoken?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the burden of Jehovah shall ye mention no more; for every man's own word shall be his burden: for ye have perverted the words of the livingGod, of Jehovah of hosts, ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus shalt thou say to the prophet: What hath Jehovah answered thee? and, What hath Jehovah spoken?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But since ye say, The burden of Jehovah, therefore thus saith Jehovah: Because ye say this word, The burden of Jehovah, and I have sent unto you, saying, Ye shall not say, The burden of Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore behold, I will utterly forget you, and I will cast you off, far from my face, and the city that I gave to you and to your fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will bring everlasting reproach upon you, and everlasting shame, that shall not be forgotten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah shewed me, and behold, two baskets of figs, set before the temple of Jehovah, after that Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon had carried away captive from Jerusalem, Jeconiah the son of Jehoiakim, the king of Judah, and the princes of Judah, and the craftsmen and smiths, and had brought them to Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One basket had very good figs, like the figs first ripe; and the other basket had very bad figs, which could not be eaten for badness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, What seest thou, Jeremiah? And I said, Figs: the good figs very good; and the bad very bad, which cannot be eaten for badness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah, theGod of Israel: Like these good figs, so will I regard for good them that are carried away captive of Judah, whom I have sent out of this place into the land of the Chaldeans;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will set mine eyes upon them for good, and I will bring them again to this land; and I will build them and not pull them down, and I will plant them and not pluck them up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give them a heart to know me, that I am Jehovah; and they shall be my people, and I will be theirGod: for they shall return unto me with their whole heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as the bad figs, which cannot be eaten for badness, surely, thus saith Jehovah: So will I make Zedekiah the king of Judah, and his princes, and the remnant of Jerusalem, that remain in this land, and them that dwell in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give them over to be driven hither and thither unto all the kingdoms of the earth for evil, to be a reproach and a proverb, a taunt and a curse, in all places whither I shall drive them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will send among them the sword, the famine, and the pestilence, until they be consumed from off the land that I gave unto them and to their fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah, in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah (that is, the first year of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which Jeremiah the prophet spoke unto all the people of Judah and to all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, saying:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the thirteenth year of Josiah the son of Amon, the king of Judah, even unto this day, these three and twenty years, the word of Jehovah hath come unto me, and I have spoken unto you, rising early and speaking; but ye have not hearkened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah hath sent unto you all his servants the prophets, rising early and sending; but ye have not hearkened, nor inclined your ear to hear,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when they said, Turn again now every one from his evil way, and from the wickedness of your doings, and dwell in the land that Jehovah hath given unto you and to your fathers from of old even for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And go not after othergods, to serve them and to worship them; and provoke me not to anger with the work of your hands; and I will do you no hurt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye have not hearkened unto me, saith Jehovah; that ye might provoke me to anger with the work of your hands, to your own hurt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Because ye have not listened to my words,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: behold, I will send and take all the families of the north, saith Jehovah, and [I will send] to Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon, my servant, and will bring them against this land, and against the inhabitants thereof, and against all these nations round about; and I will utterly destroy them, and make them an astonishment, and a hissing, and perpetual wastes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will cause to perish from them the voice of mirth and the voice of joy, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the sound of the millstones and the light of the lamp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this whole land shall become a waste, an astonishment; and these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when seventy years are accomplished, [that] I will visit on the king of Babylon and on that nation, saith Jehovah, their iniquity, and on the land of the Chaldeans, and I will make it perpetual desolations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will bring upon that land all my words which I have pronounced against it, all that is written in this book, which Jeremiah hath prophesied against all the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For many nations and great kings shall serve themselves of them also; and I will recompense them according to their deeds, and according to the work of their hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus hath Jehovah theGod of Israel said unto me: Take the cup of the wine of this fury at my hand, and cause all the nations to whom I send thee to drink it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall drink, and reel to and fro, and be mad, because of the sword that I will send among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I took the cup at Jehovah's hand, and made all the nations to drink, to whom Jehovah had sent me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jerusalem, and the cities of Judah, and the kings thereof, and the princes thereof, to make them a waste, an astonishment, a hissing, and a curse, as it is this day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh king of Egypt, and his servants, and his princes, and all his people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the mingled people, and all the kings of the land of Uz, and all the kings of the land of the Philistines, and Ashkelon, and Gazah, and Ekron, and the remnant of Ashdod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Edom, and Moab, and the children of Ammon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the kings of Tyre, and all the kings of Zidon, and the kings of the isles that are beyond the sea;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dedan, and Tema, and Buz, and all that have the corners [of their beard] cut off;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the kings of Arabia, and all the kings of the mingled people that dwell in the desert;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the kings of Zimri, and all the kings of Elam, and all the kings of the Medes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the kings of the north, far and near, one with another; and all the kingdoms of the world, which are upon the face of the earth; and the king of Sheshach shall drink after them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Drink, and be drunken, and vomit, and fall, and rise no more, because of the sword that I will send among you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, if they refuse to take the cup from thy hand to drink, then shalt thou say unto them, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Ye shall certainly drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, I begin to bring evil on the city that is called by my name, and should ye be altogether unpunished? Ye shall not be unpunished; for I call for a sword upon all the inhabitants of the earth, saith Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, prophesy unto them all these words, and say unto them, Jehovah will roar from on high, and utter his voice from his holy habitation; he will mightily roar upon his dwelling-place, he will give a shout, as they that tread [the vintage], against all the inhabitants of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The noise shall come to the end of the earth: for Jehovah hath a controversy with the nations, he entereth into judgment with all flesh; as for the wicked, he will give them up to the sword, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Behold, evil shall go forth from nation to nation, and a great storm shall be raised up from the uttermost parts of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the slain of Jehovah shall [be] at that day from [one] end of the earth even unto the [other] end of the earth: they shall not be lamented, neither gathered, nor buried; they shall be dung upon the face of the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Howl, ye shepherds, and cry; and wallow yourselves [in the dust], noble ones of the flock: for the days of your slaughter are accomplished, and I will disperse you; and ye shall fall like a precious vessel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And refuge shall perish from the shepherds, and escape from the noble ones of the flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There shall be a voice of the cry of the shepherds, and a howling of the noble ones of the flock: for Jehovah layeth waste their pasture;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the peaceable enclosures shall be desolated, because of the fierce anger of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath forsaken his covert as a young lion; for their land is a desolation because of the fierceness of the oppressor, and because of his fierce anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the beginning of the reign of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah, came this word from Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Stand in the court of Jehovah's house, and speak unto all the cities of Judah, which come to worship in Jehovah's house, all the words that I command thee to speak unto them: diminish not a word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peradventure they will hearken, and turn every man from his evil way, that I may repent me of the evil which I purpose to do unto them because of the wickedness of their doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith Jehovah: If ye will not hearken unto me, to walk in my law, which I have set before you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to hearken unto the words of my servants the prophets, whom I have sent unto you, even rising early and sending [them], but ye have not hearkened,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — then will I make this house like Shiloh, and will make this city a curse to all the nations of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priests and the prophets and all the people heard Jeremiah speaking these words in the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jeremiah had ended speaking all that Jehovah had commanded [him] to speak unto all the people, that the priests and the prophets and all the people seized him, saying, Thou shalt certainly die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why hast thou prophesied in the name of Jehovah, saying, This house shall be like Shiloh, and this city shall be desolate, without inhabitant? And all the people were gathered against Jeremiah in the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes of Judah heard these things; and they went up from the king's house unto the house of Jehovah, and sat in the entry of the new gate of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priests and the prophets spoke unto the princes and to all the people, saying, This man is worthy to die, for he hath prophesied against this city, as ye have heard with your ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah spoke to all the princes and to all the people, saying, Jehovah sent me to prophesy against this house and against this city all the words that ye have heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, amend your ways and your doings, and hearken to the voice of Jehovah yourGod; and Jehovah will repent him of the evil that he hath pronounced against you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, behold, I am in your hand; do unto me as seemeth good and right in your eyes:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: only know for certain that if ye put me to death, ye shall surely bring innocent blood upon yourselves, and upon this city, and upon the inhabitants thereof; for of a truth Jehovah hath sent me unto you to speak all these words in your ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes and all the people said unto the priests and to the prophets, This man is not worthy to die; for he hath spoken to us in the name of Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there rose up certain of the elders of the land and spoke to all the congregation of the people, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Micah the Morasthite prophesied in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah, and spoke to all the people of Judah, saying, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Zion shall be ploughed [as] a field, and Jerusalem shall become heaps, and the mountain of the house as the high places of a forest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Did Hezekiah king of Judah and all Judah put him at all to death? Did he not fear Jehovah, and supplicate Jehovah, and Jehovah repented him of the evil that he had pronounced against them? And we should be doing a great evil against our souls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was also a man that prophesied in the name of Jehovah, Urijah the son of Shemaiah of Kirjath-jearim: and he prophesied against this city and against this land according to all the words of Jeremiah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehoiakim the king, and all his mighty men, and all the princes, heard his words, and the king sought to put him to death; but Urijah heard it, and he was afraid, and fled, and went into Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehoiakim the king sent men into Egypt, Elnathan the son of Achbor, and men with him, into Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they fetched forth Urijah out of Egypt, and brought him to Jehoiakim the king; and he slew him with the sword, and cast his dead body into the graves of the children of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Nevertheless the hand of Ahikam the son of Shaphan was with Jeremiah, that they should not give him into the hand of the people to put him to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the beginning of the reign of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah, came this word to Jeremiah from Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus hath Jehovah said unto me: Make thee bonds and yokes, and put them upon thy neck;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and send them to the king of Edom, and to the king of Moab, and to the king of the children of Ammon, and to the king of Tyre, and to the king of Zidon, by the hand of the messengers that come to Jerusalem unto Zedekiah king of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And give them a charge unto their masters, saying, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Thus shall ye say unto your masters:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have made the earth, man and beast that are upon the face of the earth, by my great power and by my outstretched arm; and I give them unto whom it seemeth right in mine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now have I given all these lands into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, my servant; and the beasts of the field also have I given him to serve him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the nations shall serve him, and his son, and his son's son, until the time of his land also come, when many nations and great kings shall reduce him to servitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, that the nation and kingdom which will not serve the same Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and that will not put their neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon, that nation will I visit, saith Jehovah, with the sword, and with the famine, and with the pestilence, until I have consumed them by his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye, hearken not to your prophets, nor to your diviners, nor to your dreamers, nor to your soothsayers, nor to your sorcerers, who speak unto you saying: Ye shall not serve the king of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they prophesy falsehood unto you, to remove you far from your land, and that I should drive you out, and ye should perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the nation that bringeth its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon and serveth him, will I let remain still in its own land, saith Jehovah; and they shall till it, and dwell therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I spoke to Zedekiah king of Judah according to all these words, saying, bring your necks under the yoke of the king of Babylon, and serve him and his people, and live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why will ye die, thou and thy people, by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence, as Jehovah hath spoken concerning the nation that will not serve the king of Babylon?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And hearken not unto the words of the prophets that speak unto you, saying, Ye shall not serve the king of Babylon; for they prophesy falsehood unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have not sent them, saith Jehovah, yet they prophesy falsely in my name; in order that I should drive you out, and that ye should perish, ye, and the prophets who prophesy unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I spoke to the priests and to all this people, saying, Thus saith Jehovah: Hearken not to the words of your prophets that prophesy unto you, saying, Behold, the vessels of Jehovah's house shall now shortly be brought again from Babylon; for they prophesy falsehood unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hearken not unto them; serve the king of Babylon, and live: wherefore should this city become a waste?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if they be prophets, and if the word of Jehovah be with them, let them now make intercession to Jehovah of hosts, that the vessels which are left in the house of Jehovah, and in the house of the king of Judah, and at Jerusalem, go not to Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah of hosts concerning the pillars, and concerning the sea, and concerning the bases, and concerning the rest of the vessels that are left in this city,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon took not, when he carried away captive Jeconiah the son of Jehoiakim, the king of Judah, from Jerusalem to Babylon, along with all the nobles of Judah and Jerusalem;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: yea, thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, concerning the vessels that are left in the house of Jehovah, and in the house of the king of Judah, and in Jerusalem:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall be carried to Babylon, and there shall they be until the day of my visiting them, saith Jehovah; then I will bring them up, and restore them to this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the same year, in the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah, in the fourth year, in the fifth month, [that] Hananiah the son of Azzur, the prophet, who was of Gibeon, spoke to me in the house of Jehovah, in the presence of the priests and of all the people, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus speaketh Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, saying, I have broken the yoke of the king of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Within two full years will I bring again into this place all the vessels of Jehovah's house, that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon took away from this place, and carried to Babylon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will bring again to this place Jeconiah the son of Jehoiakim, the king of Judah, with all the captives of Judah that went to Babylon, saith Jehovah: for I will break the yoke of the king of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet Jeremiah spoke unto the prophet Hananiah in the presence of the priests, and in the presence of all the people that stood in the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet Jeremiah said, Amen, may Jehovah do so! may Jehovah perform thy words which thou hast prophesied, to bring again from Babylon, into this place, the vessels of Jehovah's house, and all them of the captivity!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless, hear, I pray thee, this word which I speak in thine ears, and in the ears of all the people:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The prophets that have been before me and before thee of old, prophesied also concerning many countries and concerning great kingdoms, of war, and of evil, and of pestilence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The prophet that prophesieth of peace, when the word of the prophet shall come to pass, shall be known as the prophet whom Jehovah hath really sent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet Hananiah took the yoke from off the prophet Jeremiah's neck, and broke it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hananiah spoke in the presence of all the people, saying, Thus saith Jehovah: So will I break the yoke of Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon within two full years from off the neck of all the nations. And the prophet Jeremiah went his way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto Jeremiah, after that the prophet Hananiah had broken the yoke from off the neck of the prophet Jeremiah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and tell Hananiah, saying, Thus saith Jehovah: Thou hast broken the yokes of wood, and thou hast made in their place yokes of iron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: I have put a yoke of iron upon the neck of all these nations, that they may serve Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon; and they shall serve him: and I have given him the beasts of the field also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet Jeremiah said unto the prophet Hananiah, Hear now, Hananiah: Jehovah hath not sent thee; and thou makest this people to trust in falsehood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will cast thee from off the face of the earth: this year thou shalt die, for thou hast spoken revolt against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet Hananiah died in the same year in the seventh month.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the words of the letter that the prophet Jeremiah sent from Jerusalem to the residue of the elders of the captivity, and to the priests, and to the prophets, and to all the people whom Nebuchadnezzar had carried away captive from Jerusalem to Babylon

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (after that Jeconiah the king, and the queen, and the eunuchs, the princes of Judah and Jerusalem, and the craftsmen, and the smiths, had departed from Jerusalem);

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: by the hand of Elasah the son of Shaphan, and Gemariah the son of Hilkijah (whom Zedekiah king of Judah sent to Babylon, unto Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon), saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, unto all the captivity, whom I have caused to be carried away from Jerusalem unto Babylon:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Build houses, and dwell in them, and plant gardens, and eat the fruit of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take wives, and beget sons and daughters; and take wives for your sons, and give your daughters to husbands, that they may bear sons and daughters; and multiply there, and be not diminished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seek the peace of the city whither I have caused you to be carried away captive, and pray unto Jehovah for it: for in the peace thereof shall ye have peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Let not your prophets that are in your midst, nor your diviners deceive you, neither hearken to your dreams, which ye like to dream.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they prophesy falsely unto you in my name: I have not sent them, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: When seventy years shall be accomplished for Babylon I will visit you, and perform my good word toward you, in bringing you back to this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith Jehovah, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you in your latter end a hope.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall call upon me, and ye shall go and pray unto me, and I will hearken unto you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye shall seek me and find me, for ye shall search for me with all your heart,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will be found of you, saith Jehovah. And I will turn your captivity, and I will gather you from all the nations, and from all the places whither I have driven you, saith Jehovah; and I will bring you again into the place whence I have caused you to be carried away captive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye say, Jehovah hath raised us up prophets in Babylon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: yea, thus saith Jehovah concerning the king that sitteth upon the throne of David, and concerning all the people that dwell in this city, your brethren that are not gone forth with you into captivity;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Behold, I will send against them the sword, the famine, and the pestilence, and will make them like the vile figs, that cannot be eaten for badness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will pursue them with the sword, with the famine, and with the pestilence, and will give them over to be driven hither and thither into all the kingdoms of the earth, to be an execration, and an astonishment, and a hissing, and a reproach, among all the nations whither I will drive them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because they have not hearkened to my words, saith Jehovah, wherewith I sent unto them my servants the prophets, rising early and sending; but ye have not hearkened, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye, all ye of the captivity, whom I have sent from Jerusalem to Babylon, hear the word of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, concerning Ahab the son of Kolaiah, and concerning Zedekiah the son of Maaseiah, who prophesy falsehood unto you in my name: Behold, I will give them into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, and he shall smite them before your eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of them shall be taken up a curse by all the captivity of Judah that are in Babylon, saying, Jehovah make thee like Zedekiah and like Ahab, whom the king of Babylon roasted in the fire;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because they have committed infamy in Israel, and have committed adultery with their neighbours' wives, and have spoken words of falsehood in my name, which I had not commanded them: and I [am] he that knoweth, and [am] witness, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt speak to Shemaiah the Nehelamite, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus speaketh Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, saying, Because thou hast sent letters in thy name unto all the people that are at Jerusalem, and to Zephaniah the son of Maaseiah the priest, and to all the priests, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath made thee priest in the stead of Jehoiada the priest, that there should be officers [in] the house of Jehovah, over every madman and self-made prophet, that thou shouldest put him in the stocks and in the shackles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, why hast thou not reproved Jeremiah of Anathoth, who maketh himself a prophet to you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forasmuch as he hath sent unto us in Babylon, saying, It will be long; build houses, and dwell [in them], and plant gardens, and eat the fruit of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And Zephaniah the priest read this letter in the ears of the prophet Jeremiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto Jeremiah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Send to all them of the captivity, saying, Thus saith Jehovah concerning Shemaiah the Nehelamite: Because that Shemaiah hath prophesied unto you, and I sent him not, and he hath caused you to trust in falsehood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will punish Shemaiah the Nehelamite, and his seed: he shall not have a man to dwell among this people; neither shall he see the good that I will do for my people, saith Jehovah; because he hath spoken revolt against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus speaketh Jehovah theGod of Israel, saying, Write thee in a book all the words that I have spoken unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, the days come, saith Jehovah, when I will turn the captivity of my people Israel and Judah, saith Jehovah; and I will cause them to return to the land that I gave to their fathers, and they shall possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the words that Jehovah hath spoken concerning Israel and concerning Judah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for thus saith Jehovah: We have heard a voice of trembling, there is fear, and no peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ask ye now, and see, whether a male doth travail with child? Wherefore do I see every man with his hands on his loins, as a woman in travail; and all faces are turned into paleness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Alas! for that day is great, so that none is like it: it is even the time of Jacob's trouble; but he shall be saved out of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass in that day, saith Jehovah of hosts, I will break his yoke from off thy neck, and will burst thy bonds, and strangers shall no more reduce him to servitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they shall serve Jehovah theirGod, and David their king, whom I will raise up unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, my servant Jacob, fear not, saith Jehovah; neither be dismayed, O Israel: for behold, I will save thee from afar, and thy seed from the land of their captivity; and Jacob shall return, and shall be in rest, and at ease, and none shall make [him] afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I am with thee, saith Jehovah, to save thee: for I will make a full end of all the nations whither I have scattered thee; yet of thee will I not make a full end, but I will correct thee with judgment, and will not hold thee altogether guiltless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: Thy bruise is incurable, thy wound is grievous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is none to plead thy cause, to bind up [thy wound]; thou hast no healing medicines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All thy lovers have forgotten thee; they seek thee not. For I have smitten thee with the stroke of an enemy, with the chastisement of a cruel one, for the greatness of thine iniquity: thy sins are manifold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why criest thou because of thy bruise? thy sorrow is incurable; for the greatness of thine iniquity, [because] thy sins are manifold, I have done these things unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore all that devour thee shall be devoured, and all thine adversaries, every one of them, shall go into captivity; and they that spoil thee shall be for a spoil; and all they that prey upon thee will I give to be a prey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I will apply a bandage unto thee, and I will heal thee of thy wounds, saith Jehovah; for they have called thee an outcast: This is Zion that no man seeketh after.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will turn the captivity of Jacob's tents, and have mercy on his habitations; and the city shall be built upon her own heap; and the palace shall be inhabited after the manner thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And out of them shall proceed thanksgiving, and the voice of them that make merry: and I will multiply them, and they shall not be diminished; and I will honour them, and they shall not be small.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their sons shall be as aforetime; and their assembly shall be established before me; and I will punish all that oppress them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their prince shall be of themselves, and their ruler shall proceed from the midst of them; and I will cause him to approach, and he shall draw near unto me. For who is this that engageth his heart to draw near unto me? saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall be my people, and I will be yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, a tempest of Jehovah, fury is gone forth, a sweeping storm: it shall whirl down upon the head of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fierce anger of Jehovah shall not return, until he have executed, and until he have performed the purposes of his heart. At the end of the days ye shall consider it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time, saith Jehovah, will I be theGod of all the families of Israel, and they shall be my people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: The people [that were] left of the sword have found grace in the wilderness, [even] Israel, when I go to give him rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath appeared from afar unto me, [saying,] Yea, I have loved thee with an everlasting love; therefore with loving-kindness have I drawn thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will build thee again, and thou shalt be built, O virgin of Israel! Thou shalt again be adorned with thy tambours, and shalt go forth in the dances of them that make merry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt again plant vineyards upon the mountains of Samaria; the planters shall plant, and shall eat the fruit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there shall be a day, when the watchmen upon mount Ephraim shall cry, Arise, and let us go up to Zion, unto Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: Sing aloud [with] gladness for Jacob, and shout at the head of the nations; publish ye, praise ye, and say, Jehovah, save thy people, the remnant of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I bring them from the north country, and gather them from the uttermost parts of the earth; [and] among them the blind and the lame, the woman with child and her that travaileth with child together: a great assemblage shall they return hither.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall come with weeping, and with supplications will I lead them; I will cause them to walk by water-brooks, in a straight way, wherein they shall not stumble; for I will be a father to Israel, and Ephraim is my firstborn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear the word of Jehovah, ye nations, and declare [it] to the isles afar off, and say, He that scattered Israel will gather him, and keep him, as a shepherd his flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah hath ransomed Jacob, and redeemed him from the hand of one stronger than he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall come and sing aloud upon the height of Zion, and shall flow together to the goodness of Jehovah, for corn, and for new wine, and for oil, and for the young of the flock and of the herd; and their soul shall be as a watered garden, and they shall not languish any more at all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall the virgin rejoice in the dance, and the young men and old together; for I will turn their mourning into gladness, and will comfort them, and make them rejoice after their sorrow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will satiate the soul of the priests with fatness, and my people shall be satisfied with my goodness, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: A voice hath been heard in Ramah, the wail of very bitter weeping, — Rachel weeping for her children, refusing to be comforted for her children, because they are not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Refrain thy voice from weeping, and thine eyes from tears; for there is a reward for thy work, saith Jehovah; and they shall come again from the land of the enemy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there is hope for thy latter end, saith Jehovah, and thy children shall come again to their own border.

translations.name: Darby Bible
