translations.text: I have indeed heard Ephraim bemoaning himself [thus]: Thou hast chastised me, and I was chastised as a bullock not trained: turn thou me, and I shall be turned; for thou art Jehovah myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely after that I was turned, I repented; and after I knew myself, I smote upon [my] thigh. I was ashamed, yea, even confounded, for I bear the reproach of my youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is Ephraim a dear son unto me? is he a child of delights? For whilst I have been speaking against him, I do constantly remember him still. Therefore my bowels are troubled for him: I will certainly have mercy upon him, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Set up waymarks, make for thyself signposts; set thy heart toward the highway, the way by which thou wentest: turn again, O virgin of Israel, turn again to these thy cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long wilt thou wander about, thou backsliding daughter? For Jehovah hath created a new thing on the earth, a woman shall encompass a man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: They shall again use this speech, in the land of Judah and in the cities thereof, when I shall turn their captivity: Jehovah bless thee, O habitation of righteousness, mountain of holiness!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And therein shall dwell Judah, and all the cities thereof together, the husbandmen, and they that go about with flocks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have satiated the weary soul, and every languishing soul have I replenished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Upon this I awaked, and beheld; and my sleep was sweet unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, days come, saith Jehovah, that I will sow the house of Israel and the house of Judah [with] the seed of man and the seed of beast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, as I have watched over them, to pluck up, and to break down, and to overthrow, and to destroy, and to afflict; so will I watch over them to build, and to plant, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days they shall say no more, The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for every one shall die for his own iniquity; every man that eateth the sour grapes, his teeth shall be set on edge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, days come, saith Jehovah, that I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers, in the day of my taking them by the hand, to lead them out of the land of Egypt; which my covenant they broke, although I was a husband unto them, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel, after those days, saith Jehovah: I will put my law in their inward parts, and will write it in their heart; and I will be theirGod, and they shall be my people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall teach no more every man his neighbour, and every man his brother, saying, Know Jehovah; for they shall all know me, from the least of them unto the greatest of them, saith Jehovah: for I will pardon their iniquity, and their sin will I remember no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah, who giveth the sun for light by day, the ordinances of the moon and of the stars for light by night, who stirreth up the sea so that the waves thereof roar, — Jehovah of hosts is his name:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If those ordinances depart from before me, saith Jehovah, the seed of Israel also shall cease from being a nation before me for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: If the heavens above can be measured, and the foundations of the earth searched out beneath, I will also cast off the whole seed of Israel, for all that they have done, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the days come, saith Jehovah, that the city shall be built to Jehovah, from the tower of Hananeel unto the corner-gate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the measuring line shall yet go forth before it unto the hill Gareb, and shall turn toward Goath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the whole valley of the dead bodies, and of the ashes, and all the fields unto the torrent Kidron, unto the corner of the horse-gate toward the east, shall be holy unto Jehovah: it shall not be plucked up, nor overthrown any more for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Jehovah in the tenth year of Zedekiah king of Judah: that year was the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Babylon's army was then besieging Jerusalem; and the prophet Jeremiah was shut up in the court of the guard, which was in the king of Judah's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Zedekiah king of Judah had shut him up, saying, Why dost thou prophesy and say, Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I give this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall take it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Zedekiah king of Judah shall not escape out of the hand of the Chaldeans; for he shall certainly be given into the hand of the king of Babylon, and shall speak with him mouth to mouth, and his eyes shall behold his eyes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he shall lead Zedekiah to Babylon, and there shall he be until I visit him, saith Jehovah: though ye fight with the Chaldeans, ye shall not prosper?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said, The word of Jehovah came unto me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, Hanameel, the son of Shallum thine uncle, shall come unto thee, saying, Buy for thyself my field which is in Anathoth; for thine is the right of redemption, to buy [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hanameel, mine uncle's son, came to me in the court of the guard according to the word of Jehovah, and said unto me, Buy my field, I pray thee, that is in Anathoth, which is in the land of Benjamin; for the right of inheritance is thine, and the redemption is thine: buy [it] for thyself. Then I knew that this was the word of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I bought of Hanameel, mine uncle's son, the field which is in Anathoth, and weighed him the money, seventeen shekels of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I subscribed the writing, and sealed it, and took witnesses, and weighed the money in the balances.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I took the writing of the purchase, that which was sealed [according to] the law and the statutes, and that which was open;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I gave the writing of the purchase unto Baruch the son of Nerijah, the son of Maaseiah, in the sight of Hanameel mine uncle's [son], and in the presence of the witnesses that had subscribed the writing of purchase, before all the Jews that were sitting in the court of the guard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I charged Baruch in their presence, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Take these writings, this writing of the purchase, both that which is sealed and this writing which is open; and put them in an earthen vessel, that they may remain many days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Houses and fields and vineyards shall again be purchased in this land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after I had given the writing of the purchase unto Baruch the son of Nerijah, I prayed unto Jehovah saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Alas, Lord Jehovah! Behold, thou hast made the heavens and the earth by thy great power and stretched-out arm; there is nothing too hard for thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who shewest mercy unto thousands, and recompensest the iniquity of the fathers into the bosom of their children after them, thou, the great, the mightyGod, — Jehovah of hosts is his name;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: great in counsel and mighty in work, whose eyes are open upon all the ways of the children of men, to give every one according to his ways, and according to the fruit of his doings:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who hast displayed signs and wonders unto this day, in the land of Egypt and in Israel and among [other] men; and hast made thee a name, as at this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou broughtest forth thy people Israel out of the land of Egypt by signs, and by wonders, and by a powerful hand, and by a stretched-out arm, and by great terror;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and didst give them this land, which thou hadst sworn unto their fathers to give them, a land flowing with milk and honey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came in and possessed it: but they hearkened not unto thy voice, neither walked in thy law; they have done nothing of all that thou commandedst them to do; so that thou hast caused all this evil to come upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold the mounds, they are come unto the city for taking it; and the city is given over into the hand of the Chaldeans, that fight against it, by the sword, and the famine, and the pestilence: and what thou hast spoken is come to pass; and behold, thou seest [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Lord Jehovah, thou hast said unto me, Buy for thyself the field for money, and take witnesses; — and the city is given into the hand of the Chaldeans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto Jeremiah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I am Jehovah, theGod of all flesh: is there anything too hard for me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I give this city into the hand of the Chaldeans, and into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon, and he shall take it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Chaldeans, that fight against this city, shall come in and set fire to this city, and shall burn it, and the houses upon whose roofs they have offered incense unto Baal, and poured out drink-offerings unto othergods, to provoke me to anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the children of Israel and the children of Judah have been doing only evil in my sight from their youth; for the children of Israel have only provoked me to anger with the work of their hands, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this city hath been to me [a provocation] of mine anger and of my fury from the day that they built it even unto this day; that I should remove it from before my face,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because of all the evil of the children of Israel and of the children of Judah, which they have done to provoke me to anger, they, their kings, their princes, their priests, and their prophets, and the men of Judah, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they have turned unto me the back, and not the face; and though I taught them, rising early and teaching, they hearkened not to receive instruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they have set their abominations in the house which is called by my name, to defile it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they have built the high places of Baal, which are in the valley of the son of Hinnom, to cause to pass through [the fire] their sons and their daughters unto Molech: which I commanded them not, neither came it into my mind, that they should do this abomination, to cause Judah to sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now therefore Jehovah, theGod of Israel, saith thus concerning this city, whereof ye say, It hath been given over into the hand of the king of Babylon by the sword, and by the famine, and by the pestilence:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will gather them out of all the countries whither I have driven them, in mine anger, and in my fury, and in great wrath; and I will bring them again unto this place, and I will cause them to dwell safely.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall be my people, and I will be theirGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give them one heart, and one way, that they may fear me all [their] days, for the good of them, and of their children after them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will make an everlasting covenant with them, that I will not draw back from them, to do them good; and I will put my fear in their heart, that they may not turn aside from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will rejoice over them to do them good, and I will assuredly plant them in this land with my whole heart and with my whole soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: Like as I have brought all this great evil upon this people, so will I bring upon them all the good that I have spoken concerning them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And fields shall be bought in this land, whereof ye say, It is desolate without man or beast; it is given into the hand of the Chaldeans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Men] shall buy fields for money, and subscribe the writings, and seal them, and take witnesses, in the land of Benjamin, and in the environs of Jerusalem, and in the cities of Judah, and in the cities of the hill-country, and in the cities of the lowland, and in the cities of the south: for I will turn their captivity, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Jeremiah the second time, while he was still shut up in the court of the prison, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah the doer of it, Jehovah that formeth it to establish it, Jehovah is his name:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Call unto me, and I will answer thee, and I will shew thee great and hidden things, which thou knowest not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel concerning the houses of this city, and concerning the houses of the kings of Judah, which are thrown down because of the mounds and because of the sword:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They come to fight with the Chaldeans, but to fill them with the dead bodies of the men whom I have slain in mine anger and in my fury, and for all whose wickedness I have hid my face from this city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will apply a healing dressing to it and cure, and I will heal them, and will reveal unto them an abundance of peace and truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will turn the captivity of Judah and the captivity of Israel, and will build them, as at the beginning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will cleanse them from all their iniquity, whereby they have sinned against me, and I will pardon all their iniquities, whereby they have sinned against me, and whereby they have transgressed against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be to me a name of joy, a praise and a glory before all the nations of the earth, which shall hear of all the good that I do unto them; and they shall fear and tremble for all the good and for all the prosperity that I procure unto it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: In this place of which ye say, It is waste, without man and without beast! in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem that are desolate, without man, and without inhabitant, and without beast,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: there shall again be heard the voice of mirth and the voice of joy, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the voice of them that say, Give ye thanks unto Jehovah of hosts; for Jehovah is good, for his loving-kindness [endureth] for ever, — of them that bring thanksgiving unto the house of Jehovah. For I will turn the captivity of the land as in the beginning, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: In this place which is waste, without man and without beast, and in all the cities thereof, there shall again be a habitation of shepherds causing [their] flocks to lie down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the cities of the hill-country, in the cities of the lowland, and in the cities of the south, and in the land of Benjamin, and in the environs of Jerusalem, and in the cities of Judah, shall the flocks pass again under the hands of him that counteth [them], saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the days come, saith Jehovah, that I will perform the good word which I have spoken unto the house of Israel and unto the house of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days, and at that time, will I cause a Branch of righteousness to grow up unto David; and he shall execute judgment and righteousness in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days shall Judah be saved, and Jerusalem shall dwell in safety. And this is the name wherewith she shall be called: Jehovah our Righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: There shall never fail to David a man to sit upon the throne of the house of Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: neither shall there fail to the priests the Levites a man before me to offer up burnt-offerings, and to burn oblations, and to do sacrifice continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Jeremiah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: If ye can break my covenant [in respect] of the day, and my covenant [in respect] of the night, so that there should not be day and night in their season,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [then] shall also my covenant be broken with David my servant, that he should not have a son to reign upon his throne; and with the Levites, the priests, my ministers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As the host of the heavens cannot be numbered, nor the sand of the sea measured, so will I multiply the seed of David my servant, and the Levites that minister unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Jeremiah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou not seen what this people have spoken, saying, The two families that Jehovah had chosen, he hath even cast them off? And they despise my people, that they should be no more a nation before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: If my covenant of day and night [stand] not, if I have not appointed the ordinances of the heavens and the earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [then] will I also cast away the seed of Jacob, and of David my servant, so as not to take of his seed to be rulers over the seed of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: for I will turn their captivity, and will have mercy on them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Jehovah, when Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and all his army, and all the kingdoms of the earth under his dominion, and all the peoples, fought against Jerusalem and against all the cities thereof, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah, theGod of Israel: Go and speak to Zedekiah king of Judah, and tell him, Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I give this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall burn it with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt not escape out of his hand, but shalt certainly be taken, and given into his hand; and thine eyes shall behold the eyes of the king of Babylon, and his mouth shall speak with thy mouth, and thou shalt go to Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, hear the word of Jehovah, O Zedekiah king of Judah. Thus saith Jehovah as to thee: Thou shalt not die by the sword;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt die in peace, and with the burnings of thy fathers, the former kings that were before thee, so shall they burn for thee; and they will lament for thee, Ah, lord! for I have spoken the word, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet Jeremiah spoke all these words unto Zedekiah king of Judah, in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Babylon's army fought against Jerusalem, and against all the cities of Judah that were left, against Lachish, and against Azekah; for these were amongst the cities of Judah, the fenced cities that were left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came unto Jeremiah from Jehovah, after that king Zedekiah had made a covenant with all the people that were at Jerusalem, to proclaim liberty unto them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that every man should let his bondman, and every man his bondmaid, the Hebrew and the Hebrewess, go free, that none should exact service of them, [that is,] of a Jew his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the princes and all the people that had entered into the covenant obeyed, every man letting his bondman and every man his bondmaid go free, that none should exact service of them any more: they obeyed, and let [them] go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But afterwards they turned, and caused the bondmen and the bondmaids whom they had let go free, to return, and brought them into subjection for bondmen and for bondmaids.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Jeremiah from Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel: I made a covenant with your fathers in the day that I brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the end of seven years ye shall let go every man his brother, a Hebrew, who hath sold himself unto thee; when he hath served thee six years, thou shalt let him go free from thee. But your fathers hearkened not unto me, neither inclined their ear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And you, ye had this day turned, and had done right in my sight, in proclaiming liberty every man to his neighbour; and ye had made a covenant before me in the house which is called by my name:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but ye have turned and profaned my name, and caused every man his bondman, and every man his bondmaid, whom ye had set at liberty at their pleasure, to return, and ye have brought them into subjection, to be unto you for bondmen and for bondmaids.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Ye have not hearkened unto me, in proclaiming liberty, every man to his brother, and every man to his neighbour: behold, I proclaim a liberty for you, saith Jehovah, to the sword, to the pestilence, and to the famine; and I will give you over to be driven hither and thither among all the kingdoms of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give the men that have transgressed my covenant, who have not performed the words of the covenant which they had made before me, — the calf which they cut in twain, and passed between the parts thereof,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — the princes of Judah and the princes of Jerusalem, the eunuchs, and the priests, and all the people of the land, that passed between the parts of the calf;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: them will I give into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of them that seek their life; and their carcases shall be food for the fowl of the heavens and for the beasts of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zedekiah king of Judah and his princes will I give into the hand of their enemies, and into the hand of them that seek their life, and into the hand of the king of Babylon's army, which are gone up from you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I command, saith Jehovah, and I will cause them to return to this city; and they shall fight against it, and take it, and burn it with fire; and I will make the cities of Judah a desolation, without inhabitant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Jehovah in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go to the house of the Rechabites, and speak with them, and bring them into the house of Jehovah, into one of the chambers, and give them wine to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I took Jaazaniah the son of Jeremiah, the son of Habazziniah, and his brethren, and all his sons, and the whole house of the Rechabites,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I brought them into the house of Jehovah, into the chamber of the sons of Hanan the son of Igdaliah, the man ofGod, which was by the chamber of the princes, which was above the chamber of Maaseiah the son of Shallum, the keeper of the threshold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I set before the sons of the house of the Rechabites bowls full of wine, and cups, and I said unto them, Drink wine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, We will drink no wine; for Jonadab the son of Rechab our father commanded us, saying, Ye shall drink no wine, ye nor your sons for ever;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: neither shall ye build house, nor sow seed, nor plant vineyard, nor shall ye have [any]; but all your days ye shall dwell in tents, that ye may live many days in the land where ye sojourn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we have hearkened unto the voice of Jonadab the son of Rechab our father in all that he commanded us, to drink no wine all our days, we, our wives, our sons, and our daughters,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and not to build houses for us to dwell in; neither have we vineyard, nor field, nor seed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but we have dwelt in tents, and have obeyed and done according to all that Jonadab our father commanded us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon came up into the land, that we said, Come and let us go into Jerusalem because of the army of the Chaldeans, and because of the army of Syria; and we dwell at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto Jeremiah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Go and say to the men of Judah and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, Will ye not receive instruction to hearken unto my words? saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of Jonadab the son of Rechab, that he commanded his sons not to drink wine, are performed; and to this day they have drunk none, for they have obeyed their father's commandment. But I have spoken unto you, rising early and speaking, and ye have not hearkened unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have sent unto you all my servants the prophets, rising early and sending, saying, Return ye now every man from his evil way, and amend your doings, and go not after othergods to serve them; and ye shall dwell in the land that I have given to you and to your fathers: but ye have not inclined your ear nor hearkened unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, the sons of Jonadab the son of Rechab have performed the commandment of their father which he commanded them, but this people hath not hearkened unto me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore thus saith Jehovah theGod of hosts, theGod of Israel: Behold, I will bring upon Judah and upon all the inhabitants of Jerusalem all the evil that I have pronounced against them, because I have spoken unto them, but they have not hearkened, and I have called unto them, but they have not answered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said unto the house of the Rechabites, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Because ye have obeyed the commandment of Jonadab your father, and kept all his injunctions, and have done according unto all that he hath commanded you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, There shall not fail to Jonadab the son of Rechab a man to stand before me, for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah, [that] this word came to Jeremiah from Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take thee a roll of a book, and write therein all the words that I have spoken unto thee against Israel, and against Judah, and against all the nations, from the day I spoke unto thee, from the days of Josiah, even unto this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It may be the house of Judah will hear all the evil that I purpose to do unto them, that they may return every man from his evil way, and that I may forgive their iniquity and their sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah called Baruch the son of Nerijah; and Baruch wrote from the mouth of Jeremiah all the words of Jehovah, which he had spoken unto him, upon a roll of a book.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah commanded Baruch, saying, I am shut up, I cannot go into the house of Jehovah; but go thou in,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and read in the roll, which thou hast written from my mouth, the words of Jehovah in the ears of the people in the house of Jehovah upon the fast day; and thou shalt also read them in the ears of all Judah that come from their cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It may be they will present their supplication before Jehovah, and that they will return every one from his evil way; for great is the anger and the fury that Jehovah hath pronounced against this people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Baruch the son of Nerijah did according to all that the prophet Jeremiah commanded him, reading in the book the words of Jehovah in Jehovah's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the fifth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah, in the ninth month, [that] they proclaimed a fast before Jehovah, for all the people in Jerusalem, and for all the people that came from the cities of Judah to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Baruch read in the book the words of Jeremiah in the house of Jehovah, in the chamber of Gemariah the son of Shaphan the scribe, in the upper court, at the entry of the new gate of the house of Jehovah, in the ears of all the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Micah the son of Gemariah the son of Shaphan heard out of the book all the words of Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he went down to the king's house, into the scribe's chamber, and behold, all the princes were sitting there: Elishama the scribe, and Delaiah the son of Shemaiah, and Elnathan the son of Achbor, and Gemariah the son of Shaphan, and Zedekiah the son of Hananiah, and all the princes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Micah declared unto them all the words that he had heard, when Baruch read in the book in the ears of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the princes sent Jehudi the son of Nethaniah, the son of Shelemiah, the son of Cushi, unto Baruch, saying, Take in thy hand the roll in which thou hast read in the ears of the people, and come. And Baruch the son of Nerijah took the roll in his hand, and came unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said unto him, Sit down now, and read it in our ears. And Baruch read [it] in their ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when they heard all the words, they turned in fear one toward another, and said unto Baruch, We will certainly report to the king all these words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they asked Baruch, saying, Tell us now, How didst thou write all these words from his mouth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Baruch said unto them, He pronounced all these words unto me with his mouth, and I wrote [them] with ink in the book.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes said unto Baruch, Go, hide thee, thou and Jeremiah; that none may know where ye are.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went in unto the king into the court, but they laid up the roll in the chamber of Elishama the scribe; and they told all the words in the ears of the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king sent Jehudi to fetch the roll, and he fetched it out of the chamber of Elishama the scribe. And Jehudi read it in the ears of the king, and in the ears of all the princes that stood beside the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the king was sitting in the winter-house in the ninth month, and with the fire-pan burning before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, that when Jehudi had read three or four columns, he cut it with the scribe's knife, and cast it into the fire that was in the pan until all the roll was consumed in the fire that was in the pan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were not afraid, nor rent their garments, [neither] the king nor any of his servants that heard all these words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover, Elnathan and Delaiah and Gemariah had made intercession to the king that he would not burn the roll; but he would not hear them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king commanded Jerahmeel the son of Hammelech, and Seraiah the son of Azriel, and Shelemiah the son of Abdeel, to take Baruch the scribe and Jeremiah the prophet; but Jehovah hid them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after that the king had burned the roll, and the words that Baruch wrote at the mouth of Jeremiah, the word of Jehovah came to Jeremiah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take thee again another roll, and write in it all the former words that were in the first roll which Jehoiakim the king of Judah hath burned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say to Jehoiakim king of Judah, Thus saith Jehovah: Thou hast burned this roll, saying, Why hast thou written therein, saying, The king of Babylon shall certainly come and destroy this land, and shall cause to cease from it man and beast?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah: He shall have none to sit upon the throne of David; and his dead body shall be cast out in the day to the heat, and in the night to the frost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will visit their iniquity upon him, and upon his seed, and upon his servants; and I will bring upon them, and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and upon the men of Judah, all the evil that I have pronounced against them; and they have not hearkened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah took another roll, and gave it to Baruch the scribe, the son of Nerijah; and he wrote therein from the mouth of Jeremiah, all the words of the book that Jehoiakim king of Judah had burned in the fire; and there were added besides unto them many like words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Zedekiah the son of Josiah reigned instead of Coniah the son of Jehoiakim, Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon having made him king in the land of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And neither he, nor his servants, nor the people of the land, hearkened unto the words of Jehovah, which he had spoken through the prophet Jeremiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zedekiah the king sent Jehucal the son of Shelemiah, and Zephaniah the son of Maaseiah the priest to the prophet Jeremiah, saying, Pray now unto Jehovah ourGod for us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah came in and went out among the people; for they had not put him into prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh's army was come forth out of Egypt; and when the Chaldeans that besieged Jerusalem heard tidings of them, they went up from Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto Jeremiah the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel: Thus shall ye say to the king of Judah, that sent you unto me to inquire of me: Behold, Pharaoh's army, which is come forth to help you, shall return to Egypt into their own land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Chaldeans shall come again, and fight against this city, and take it, and burn it with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Deceive not yourselves, saying, The Chaldeans are certainly gone away from us; for they are not gone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For though ye had smitten the whole army of the Chaldeans that fight against you, and there remained [but] wounded men among them, [yet] should they rise up every man in his tent, and burn this city with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the army of the Chaldeans was gone up from Jerusalem because of Pharaoh's army,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Jeremiah went forth out of Jerusalem to go into the land of Benjamin, to have his portion there among the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was in the gate of Benjamin, a captain of the guard was there whose name was Irijah, the son of Shelemiah, the son of Hananiah; and he laid hold on the prophet Jeremiah, saying, Thou art deserting to the Chaldeans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said, It is false: I am not deserting to the Chaldeans. But he hearkened not to him; and Irijah laid hold on Jeremiah, and brought him to the princes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes were wroth with Jeremiah, and smote him, and put him in the place of confinement in the house of Jonathan the scribe: for they had made that the prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Jeremiah was come into the dungeon and into the vaults, and Jeremiah had remained there many days,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: king Zedekiah sent and took him out. And the king asked of him secretly in his house, and said, Is there any word from Jehovah? And Jeremiah said, There is; and he said, Thou shalt be given into the hand of the king of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said unto king Zedekiah, What have I offended against thee, or against thy servants, or against this people, that ye have put me in the prison?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And where are your prophets that prophesied unto you, saying, The king of Babylon shall not come against you, nor against this land?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now hear, I pray thee, my lord, O king: let my supplication, I pray thee, come before thee; and cause me not to return into the house of Jonathan the scribe, lest I die there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Zedekiah the king commanded, and they committed Jeremiah into the court of the guard, and they gave him daily a loaf of bread out of the bakers' street, until all the bread in the city was spent. And Jeremiah abode in the court of the guard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Shephatiah the son of Mattan, and Gedaliah the son of Pashur, and Jucal the son of Shelemiah, and Pashur the son of Malchijah, heard the words that Jeremiah had spoken unto all the people, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: He that remaineth in this city shall die by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence; but he that goeth forth to the Chaldeans shall live; and he shall have his life for a prey, and shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: This city shall certainly be given into the hand of the king of Babylon's army, and he shall take it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes said unto the king, Let this man, we pray thee, be put to death; for why should he weaken the hands of the men of war that remain in this city, and the hands of all the people, in speaking to them according to these words? for this man seeketh not the welfare of this people, but the hurt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Zedekiah said, Behold, he is in your hand; for the king is not he that can do a thing against you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they took Jeremiah, and cast him into the dungeon of Malchijah the son of Hammelech, which was in the court of the guard, and they let down Jeremiah with cords. And in the dungeon there was no water, but mire; and Jeremiah sank in the mire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ebed-melech the Ethiopian, a eunuch who was in the king's house, heard that they had put Jeremiah in the dungeon — now the king was sitting in the gate of Benjamin,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — and Ebed-melech went forth out of the king's house, and spoke to the king, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My lord, O king, these men have done evil in all that they have done to the prophet Jeremiah, whom they have cast into the dungeon; and he will die by reason of the famine in the place where he is; for there is no more bread in the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king commanded Ebed-melech the Ethiopian, saying, Take from hence thirty men with thee, and take up Jeremiah the prophet out of the dungeon, before he die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ebed-melech took the men under his order, and went into the house of the king under the treasury, and took thence old shreds and worn-out clothes, and let them down by cords into the dungeon to Jeremiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ebed-melech the Ethiopian said to Jeremiah, Put, I pray, [these] old shreds and rags under thine armholes under the cords. And Jeremiah did so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they drew up Jeremiah with cords, and brought him up out of the dungeon; and Jeremiah remained in the court of the guard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Zedekiah sent and took the prophet Jeremiah unto him, into the third entry that is in the house of Jehovah; and the king said unto Jeremiah, I will ask thee a thing: hide nothing from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said unto Zedekiah, If I declare [it] unto thee, wilt thou not certainly put me to death? and if I give thee counsel, thou wilt not hearken unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Zedekiah swore secretly unto Jeremiah, saying, [As] Jehovah liveth, that made us this soul, I will not put thee to death, neither will I give thee into the hand of these men that seek thy life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said unto Zedekiah, Thus saith Jehovah theGod of hosts, theGod of Israel: If thou wilt freely go forth to the king of Babylon's princes, then thy soul shall live, and this city shall not be burned with fire; and thou shalt live, and thy house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if thou wilt not go forth to the king of Babylon's princes, then shall this city be given into the hand of the Chaldeans, and they shall burn it with fire, and thou shalt not escape out of their hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Zedekiah said unto Jeremiah, I am afraid of the Jews that have deserted to the Chaldeans, lest they give me over into their hand, and they mock me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said, They shall not give [thee] over. Hearken, I beseech thee, unto the voice of Jehovah, in that which I speak unto thee; so shall it be well unto thee, and thy soul shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if thou refuse to go forth, this is the word which Jehovah hath shewn me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, all the women that are left in the king of Judah's house shall be brought forth to the king of Babylon's princes; and they shall say, Thy familiar friends have set thee on, and have prevailed over thee; thy feet are sunk in the mire, they are turned away back.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall bring out all thy wives and thy children to the Chaldeans, and thou shalt not escape out of their hand; for thou shalt be taken by the hand of the king of Babylon, and thou shalt cause this city to be burned with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zedekiah said unto Jeremiah, Let no man know of these words, and thou shalt not die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if the princes hear that I have talked with thee, and they come unto thee, and say unto thee, Declare unto us now what thou hast said unto the king, hide it not from us, and we will not put thee to death; and what hath the king said unto thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then thou shalt say unto them, I presented my supplication before the king, that he would not cause me to return to Jonathan's house, to die there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And all the princes came to Jeremiah, and asked him; and he told them according to all these words that the king had commanded. And they withdrew quietly from him; for the matter was not reported.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah remained in the court of the guard until the day that Jerusalem was taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jerusalem was taken, in the ninth year of Zedekiah king of Judah, in the tenth month, came Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon and all his army against Jerusalem, and they besieged it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the eleventh year of Zedekiah, in the fourth month, on the ninth of the month, the city was broken into;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the princes of the king of Babylon came in, and sat in the middle gate: Nergal-sharezer, Samgar-nebu, Sarsechim, chief chamberlain, Nergal-sharezer, chief magian, and all the rest of the princes of the king of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Zedekiah the king of Judah and all the men of war saw them, that they fled, and went forth out of the city by night, by the way of the king's garden, by the gate between the two walls; and he went out the way of the plain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the army of the Chaldeans pursued after them, and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and they took him, and brought him up to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, unto Riblah in the land of Hamath; and he pronounced judgment upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah in Riblah before his eyes, and the king of Babylon slaughtered all the nobles of Judah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he put out the eyes of Zedekiah, and bound him with chains of brass, to carry him to Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Chaldeans burned the king's house and the houses of the people with fire, and broke down the walls of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard carried away captive into Babylon the rest of the people that were left in the city, and the deserters that had deserted to him, with the rest of the people that were left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard left [certain] of the people, the poor who had nothing, in the land of Judah, and gave them vineyards and fields at the same time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon had given charge concerning Jeremiah by Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take him, and keep an eye upon him, and do him no harm; but do unto him even as he shall say unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard sent, and Nebushazban, chief chamberlain, and Nergal-sharezer, chief magian, and all the king of Babylon's princes,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: even they sent, and took Jeremiah out of the court of the guard and committed him to Gedaliah the son of Ahikam the son of Shaphan, that he should conduct him away home. And he dwelt among the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came unto Jeremiah, while he was shut up in the court of the guard, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and speak to Ebed-melech the Ethiopian, saying, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Behold, I will bring my words upon this city for evil, and not for good, and they shall come to pass before thy face in that day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will deliver thee in that day, saith Jehovah; and thou shalt not be given into the hand of the men of whom thou art afraid;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I will certainly save thee, and thou shalt not fall by the sword, but thou shalt have thy life for a prey; for thou hast put thy confidence in me, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Jehovah, after that Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard had let him go from Ramah, when he had taken him, being bound in chains, among all the captivity of Jerusalem and Judah, that were carried away captive to Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the captain of the body-guard took Jeremiah, and said unto him, Jehovah thyGod pronounced this evil upon this place,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehovah hath brought [it about] and done according as he said; for ye have sinned against Jehovah, and have not hearkened unto his voice, therefore this thing is come upon you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, behold, I loose thee this day from the chains that are upon thy hand. If it seem good in thy sight to come with me to Babylon, come, and I will keep mine eye upon thee; but if it seem ill unto thee to come with me to Babylon, forbear. See, all the land is before thee: whither it seemeth good and right in thy sight to go, thither go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And while he had not yet given answer; [he said,] Yea, go back to Gedaliah the son of Ahikam the son of Shaphan, whom the king of Babylon hath appointed over the cities of Judah, and abide with him in the midst of the people; or go wheresoever it seemeth right in thy sight to go. And the captain of the body-guard gave him provisions and a present, and let him go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah came unto Gedaliah the son of Ahikam to Mizpah, and abode with him among the people that remained in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the captains of the forces that were in the fields, they and their men, heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah the son of Ahikam over the land, and had committed unto him men, and women, and children, and of the poor of the land, of them that had not been carried away captive to Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came to Gedaliah to Mizpah; even Ishmael the son of Nethaniah, and Johanan and Jonathan the sons of Kareah, and Seraiah the son of Tanhumeth, and the sons of Ephai the Netophathite, and Jezaniah the son of a Maachathite, they and their men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gedaliah the son of Ahikam the son of Shaphan swore unto them and to their men, saying, Fear not to serve the Chaldeans: dwell in the land, and serve the king of Babylon, and it shall be well with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as for me, behold, I dwell at Mizpah, to stand before the Chaldeans, who will come unto us; and ye, gather wine, and summer fruits, and oil, and put [them] in your vessels, and dwell in your cities which ye have taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Likewise all the Jews that were in Moab, and among the children of Ammon and in Edom, and that were in all the lands, heard that the king of Babylon had left a remnant in Judah, and that he had appointed over them Gedaliah the son of Ahikam the son of Shaphan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the Jews returned out of all the places whither they had been driven, and came to the land of Judah to Gedaliah, unto Mizpah, and gathered wine and summer fruits in great abundance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the captains of the forces that were in the fields, came to Gedaliah unto Mizpah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said unto him, Dost thou indeed know that Baalis the king of the children of Ammon hath sent Ishmael the son of Nethaniah to smite thee to death? But Gedaliah the son of Ahikam believed them not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Johanan the son of Kareah spoke to Gedaliah in Mizpah secretly, saying, Let me go, I pray thee, and I will smite Ishmael the son of Nethaniah and no man shall know it: why should he take thy life, and all they of Judah who are gathered unto thee be scattered, and the remnant of Judah perish?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Gedaliah the son of Ahikam said unto Johanan the son of Kareah, Thou shalt not do this thing; for thou speakest falsely of Ishmael.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the seventh month, that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah the son of Elishama, of the royal seed, and [one] of the king's chief men, and ten men with him, came to Gedaliah the son of Ahikam unto Mizpah, and there they ate bread together, in Mizpah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ishmael the son of Nethaniah arose, and the ten men that were with him, and smote Gedaliah the son of Ahikam the son of Shaphan with the sword, and killed him, whom the king of Babylon had appointed over the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ishmael smote all the Jews that were with him, with Gedaliah, at Mizpah, and the Chaldeans that were found there, the men of war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the second day after he had killed Gedaliah, and no man knew it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that there came men from Shechem, from Shiloh, and from Samaria, eighty men, having their beards shaven and their clothes rent, and having cut themselves; with oblations and incense in their hand, to bring them to the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ishmael the son of Nethaniah went forth from Mizpah to meet them, weeping all along as he went; and it came to pass when he met them, he said unto them, Come to Gedaliah the son of Ahikam.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was so, when they came into the midst of the city, that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah, he and the men that were with him, slew them, [and cast them] into the midst of the pit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ten men were found among them that said unto Ishmael, Do not kill us, for we have hidden stores in the field, of wheat, and of barley, and of oil, and of honey. So he forbore, and did not kill them among their brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the pit into which Ishmael had cast all the dead bodies of the men whom he had slain by the side of Gedaliah was the one which Asa the king had made for fear of Baasha king of Israel: Ishmael the son of Nethaniah filled it with the slain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ishmael carried away captive all the remnant of the people that were in Mizpah, the king's daughters, and all the people that remained in Mizpah, whom Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard had committed to Gedaliah the son of Ahikam: Ishmael the son of Nethaniah carried them away captive, and departed to go over to the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the captains of the forces that were with him, heard of all the evil that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah had done;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they took all the men, and went to fight with Ishmael the son of Nethaniah, and found him by the great waters that are at Gibeon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when all the people that were with Ishmael saw Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the captains of the forces that were with him, then they were glad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people that Ishmael had carried away captive from Mizpah turned about and came back, and went to Johanan the son of Kareah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Ishmael the son of Nethaniah escaped from Johanan with eight men, and went to the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the captains of the forces that were with him, took all the remnant of the people whom he had recovered from Ishmael the son of Nethaniah, from Mizpah, after he had slain Gedaliah the son of Ahikam, the mighty men of war, and the women, and the children, and the eunuchs, whom he had brought again from Gibeon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they departed, and dwelt at Geruth-Chimham, which is by Bethlehem, to go to enter into Egypt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because of the Chaldeans; for they feared them, because Ishmael the son of Nethaniah had smitten Gedaliah the son of Ahikam, whom the king of Babylon had appointed over the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the captains of the forces, and Johanan the son of Kareah, and Jezaniah the son of Hoshaiah, and all the people from the least even to the greatest,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: came near and said unto Jeremiah the prophet, Let, we beseech thee, our supplication come before thee, and pray for us unto Jehovah thyGod, for all this remnant (for we are left a few of many, as thine eyes do behold us);

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Jehovah thyGod may shew us the way wherein we should walk, and the thing that we should do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah the prophet said unto them, I have heard; behold, I will pray unto Jehovah yourGod according to your words; and it shall come to pass [that] whatsoever thing Jehovah shall answer you, I will declare it unto you: I will keep nothing back from you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to Jeremiah, Jehovah be a true and faithful witness amongst us, if we do not even according to all the word for which Jehovah thyGod shall send thee to us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whether it be good or whether it be evil, we will hearken unto the voice of Jehovah ourGod, to whom we send thee; that it may be well with us when we hearken unto the voice of Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of ten days, that the word of Jehovah came unto Jeremiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he called Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the captains of the forces that were with him, and all the people from the least even unto the greatest,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said unto them, Thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel, to whom ye sent me to present your supplication before him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye will still abide in this land, then will I build you, and not overthrow [you], and I will plant you, and not pluck [you] up; for I repent me of the evil that I have done unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not afraid of the king of Babylon, of whom ye are afraid: be not afraid of him, saith Jehovah; for I will be with you to save you, and to deliver you from his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will grant mercies to you, that he may have mercy upon you, and cause you to return to your own land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye say, We will not dwell in this land; so as not to hearken unto the voice of Jehovah yourGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, No; but we will go into the land of Egypt, where we shall see no war, nor hear the sound of the trumpet, nor have hunger for bread; and there will we dwell;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — and now, therefore, hear the word of Jehovah, ye remnant of Judah: thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: If ye really set your faces to enter into Egypt, and go to sojourn there,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then it shall come to pass, that the sword which ye fear shall overtake you there in the land of Egypt, and the famine, whereof ye are afraid, shall follow hard after you there in Egypt; and there ye shall die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be that all the men that have set their faces to go into Egypt to sojourn there shall die by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence; and none of them shall remain or escape from the evil that I will bring upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: As mine anger and my fury have been poured forth upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, so shall my fury be poured forth upon you, when ye shall enter into Egypt; and ye shall be an execration, and an astonishment, and a curse, and a reproach, and ye shall see this place no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath said concerning you, the remnant of Judah, Go ye not into Egypt. Know certainly that I have admonished you this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye deceived yourselves in your own souls, when ye sent me unto Jehovah yourGod, saying, Pray for us unto Jehovah ourGod; and according to all that Jehovah ourGod shall say, so declare unto us, and we will do it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have this day declared [it] to you; but ye have not obeyed the voice of Jehovah yourGod, nor anything for which he hath sent me unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now know certainly that ye shall die by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence, in the place whither ye desire to go to sojourn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Jeremiah had ended speaking unto all the people all the words of Jehovah theirGod, with which Jehovah theirGod had sent him to them — all these words,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — then spoke Azariah the son of Hoshaiah, and Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the proud men, saying to Jeremiah, Thou speakest falsely: Jehovah ourGod hath not sent thee to say, Go not into Egypt to sojourn there;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Baruch the son of Nerijah is setting thee on against us, to deliver us into the hand of the Chaldeans, that they may put us to death, and carry us away captives into Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the captains of the forces, and all the people, hearkened not unto the voice of Jehovah to abide in the land of Judah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the captains of the forces, took all the remnant of Judah, that were returned from all nations whither they had been driven, to sojourn in the land of Judah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: men, and women, and children, and the king's daughters, and every person that Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard had left with Gedaliah the son of Ahikam the son of Shaphan, and Jeremiah the prophet, and Baruch the son of Nerijah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they came into the land of Egypt: for they hearkened not unto the voice of Jehovah. And they came as far as Tahpanhes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Jeremiah in Tahpanhes, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take great stones in thy hand, and hide them in the clay in the brick-kiln, which is at the entry of Pharaoh's house in Tahpanhes, in the sight of the Jews,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and say unto them, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Behold, I will send and take Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon, my servant, and will set his throne upon these stones which I have hidden, and he shall spread his royal pavilion over them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall come and smite the land of Egypt: such as are for death to death, and such as are for captivity to captivity; and such as are for the sword to the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will kindle a fire in the houses of thegods of Egypt, and he shall burn them, and carry them away captive; and he shall array himself with the land of Egypt, as a shepherd putteth on his garment; and he shall go forth from thence in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall break the pillars of Beth-shemesh, which is in the land of Egypt; and the houses of thegods of Egypt shall he burn with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the Jews who dwelt in the land of Egypt, who dwelt at Migdol, and at Tahpanhes, and at Noph, and in the country of Pathros, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Ye have seen all the evil that I have brought upon Jerusalem, and upon all the cities of Judah; and behold they are, this day, a waste, and no man dwelleth therein,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because of their wickedness which they have committed to provoke me to anger, in that they went to burn incense to serve othergods which they knew not, they, [nor] ye, nor your fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I sent unto you all my servants the prophets, rising early and sending, saying, Oh, do not this abominable thing which I hate!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they hearkened not, nor inclined their ear to turn from their wickedness, to burn no incense unto othergods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my fury and mine anger was poured forth, and was kindled in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem; and they are become a waste, a desolation, as at this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now thus saith Jehovah theGod of hosts, theGod of Israel: Wherefore commit ye great evil against your souls, to cut off from you man and woman, infant and suckling, out of the midst of Judah, to leave you no remnant;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: provoking me to anger with the works of your hands, burning incense unto othergods in the land of Egypt, whither ye are come to sojourn, that ye should be cut off, and that ye should be a curse and a reproach among all the nations of the earth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have ye forgotten the wickedness of your fathers, and the wickedness of the kings of Judah, and the wickedness of their wives, and your own wickedness, and the wickedness of your wives, which they have committed in the land of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are not humbled unto this day, neither have they feared, nor walked in my law, nor in my statutes which I set before you and before your fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Behold, I will set my face against you for evil, and to cut off all Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will take the remnant of Judah, that have set their faces to enter into the land of Egypt to sojourn there, and they shall all be consumed: in the land of Egypt shall they fall; they shall be consumed by the sword [and] by the famine, from the least even unto the greatest; they shall die by the sword and by the famine, and they shall be an execration, an astonishment, and a curse, and a reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will punish them that dwell in the land of Egypt as I have punished Jerusalem, by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and none of the remnant of Judah, that have come into the land of Egypt to sojourn there, shall escape or remain, so as to return into the land of Judah, whither they have a desire to return to dwell there; for none shall return but such as shall escape.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then all the men who knew that their wives burned incense to othergods, and all the women who stood by, a great assemblage, even all the people that dwelt in the land of Egypt, in Pathros, answered Jeremiah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As for] the word that thou hast spoken unto us in the name of Jehovah, we will not hearken unto thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but we will certainly do every word that is gone forth out of our mouth, to burn incense to the queen of the heavens, and to pour out drink-offerings to her, as we have done, we and our fathers, our kings and our princes, in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem; and we had plenty of bread, and were well, and saw no evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But since we left off burning incense to the queen of the heavens, and pouring out drink-offerings to her, we have wanted everything, and have been consumed by the sword and by the famine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when we burned incense to the queen of the heavens and poured out drink-offerings to her, did we make for her cakes to portray her, and pour out drink-offerings to her, without our husbands?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said unto all the people, to the men, and to the women, and to all the people that had given him that answer, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is it not the incense that ye burned in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem, ye and your fathers, your kings and your princes and the people of the land, that Jehovah remembered, and that came into his mind?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah could no longer bear, because of the evil of your doings, [and] because of the abominations that ye had committed; and your land is become a waste, and an astonishment, and a curse, without inhabitant, as at this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because ye have burned incense, and because ye have sinned against Jehovah, and have not hearkened unto the voice of Jehovah, nor walked in his law, nor in his statutes, nor in his testimonies; therefore this evil hath come upon you, as at this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said unto all the people, and to all the women, Hear ye the word of Jehovah, all Judah that are in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, saying, Ye and your wives have both spoken with your mouths, and fulfilled with your hands, saying, We will certainly perform our vows which we have vowed, to burn incense to the queen of the heavens, and to pour out drink-offerings unto her. Ye will certainly establish your vows, and entirely perform your vows.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore hear ye the word of Jehovah, all Judah that dwell in the land of Egypt: Behold, I have sworn by my great name, saith Jehovah, that my name shall no more be named in the mouth of any man of Judah in all the land of Egypt, saying, [As] the Lord Jehovah liveth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will watch over them for evil, and not for good; and all the men of Judah that are in the land of Egypt shall be consumed by the sword and by the famine, until there be an end of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that escape the sword shall return out of the land of Egypt into the land of Judah, a very small company; and all the remnant of Judah, that have come into the land of Egypt to sojourn there, shall know whose word shall stand, mine or theirs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this shall be the sign unto you, saith Jehovah, that I will punish you in this place, that ye may know that my words shall certainly stand against you for evil:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will give Pharaoh-Hophra king of Egypt into the hand of his enemies, and into the hand of them that seek his life; as I gave Zedekiah king of Judah into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, his enemy, and that sought his life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that Jeremiah the prophet spoke to Baruch the son of Nerijah, when he wrote these words in a book at the mouth of Jeremiah, in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah, theGod of Israel, concerning thee, Baruch:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst say, Woe unto me! for Jehovah hath added grief to my sorrow; I am weary with my sighing, and I find no rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus shalt thou say unto him, Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, what I have built do I overthrow, and what I have planted I pluck up, even this whole land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seekest thou great things for thyself? seek [them] not; for behold, I will bring evil upon all flesh, saith Jehovah; but thy life will I give unto thee for a prey in all places whither thou shalt go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word of Jehovah that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Egypt: concerning the army of Pharaoh-Necho king of Egypt, which was by the river Euphrates at Carchemish, which Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon smote in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, the king of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make ready buckler and shield, and draw near to battle!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Harness the horses, and mount ye horsemen, and stand forth with helmets; polish the spears, put on the coats of mail!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why do I see them dismayed, turned away back? And their mighty ones are beaten down, and take to flight, and look not back? Terror [is] on every side, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not the swift flee away, neither let the mighty man escape! — Toward the north, hard by the river Euphrates, they have stumbled and fallen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is this [that] riseth up as the Nile, whose waters toss themselves like the rivers?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is Egypt that riseth up as the Nile, and [his] waters toss themselves like the rivers; and he saith, I will rise up, I will cover the earth; I will destroy the city and the inhabitants thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go up, ye horses, and drive furiously, ye chariots; and let the mighty men go forth: Cush and Phut that handle the shield, and the Ludim that handle the bow [and] bend it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
