translations.text: For this is the day of the Lord Jehovah of hosts, a day of vengeance, that he may be avenged of his adversaries; and the sword shall devour, and it shall be sated and made drunk with their blood; for the Lord Jehovah of hosts hath a sacrifice in the north country, by the river Euphrates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go up to Gilead, and fetch balm, O virgin-daughter of Egypt! In vain shalt thou multiply remedies: there is no healing for thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The nations have heard of thy shame, and thy cry hath filled the earth; for the mighty man stumbleth against the mighty, they are both fallen together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that Jehovah spoke to Jeremiah the prophet, concerning the coming of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon to smite the land of Egypt:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Declare in Egypt, and publish in Migdol, and publish in Noph, and in Tahpanhes; say, Stand fast, and prepare thee; for the sword devoureth round about thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why are thy valiants swept away? They stood not, for Jehovah did thrust them down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He made many to stumble, yea, one fell upon another; and they said, Arise, and let us return to our own people and to the land of our nativity, from the oppressing sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There did they cry, Pharaoh king of Egypt is but a noise; he hath let the time appointed go by.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] I live, saith the King, whose name is Jehovah of hosts, surely as Tabor among the mountains, and as Carmel by the sea, so shall he come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, inhabitress, daughter of Egypt, furnish for thyself a captive's baggage, for Noph shall be a desolation and shall be ruined, so that none shall dwell therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Egypt is a very fair heifer; the gad-fly cometh, it cometh from the north.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also her hired men in the midst of her are like fatted bullocks; for they also have turned back, they have fled away together, they did not stand; for the day of their calamity is come upon them, the time of their visitation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her voice shall go like a serpent's; for they shall march with an army, and come against her with axes, as hewers of wood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall cut down her forest, saith Jehovah, though it be impenetrable; for they are more than the locusts, and are innumerable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The daughter of Egypt is put to shame; she is delivered into the hand of the people of the north.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, saith, Behold, I will punish Amon of No, and Pharaoh, and Egypt, and hergods, and her kings; yea, Pharaoh and them that confide in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give them into the hand of those that seek their life, and into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of his servants; but afterwards it shall be inhabited, as in the days of old, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, my servant Jacob, fear not, neither be dismayed, Israel: for behold, I will save thee from afar, and thy seed from the land of their captivity; and Jacob shall return, and be in rest and at ease, and none shall make [him] afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear thou not, my servant Jacob, saith Jehovah: for I am with thee; for I will make a full end of all the nations whither I have driven thee, but I will not make a full end of thee; but I will correct thee with judgment, and I will not hold thee altogether guiltless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word of Jehovah that came to the prophet Jeremiah concerning the Philistines, before Pharaoh smote Gazah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, waters rise up out of the north, and shall become an overflowing flood, and shall overflow the land, and all that is therein; the city, and them that dwell therein: and the men shall cry, and all the inhabitants of the land shall howl,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: at the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his steeds, at the rushing of his chariots, at the rumbling of his wheels: fathers shall not look back for [their] children, from feebleness of hands;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because of the day that cometh to lay waste all the Philistines, to cut off from Tyre and Sidon every helper that remaineth; for Jehovah will lay waste the Philistines, the remnant of the island of Caphtor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Baldness is come upon Gazah; Ashkelon is cut off, the remnant of their valley: how long wilt thou cut thyself?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Alas! sword of Jehovah, how long wilt thou not be quiet? Withdraw into thy scabbard, rest, and be still.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How shouldest thou be quiet? — For Jehovah hath given it a charge: against Ashkelon, and against the sea shore, there hath he appointed it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Concerning Moab. Thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Woe unto Nebo! for it is spoiled; Kirjathaim is put to shame, it is taken; Misgab is put to shame and dismayed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moab's praise is no more; in Heshbon they have devised evil against her: Come, and let us cut her off from [being] a nation. Thou also, O Madmen, shalt be cut down; the sword shall pursue thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A voice of crying from Horonaim; wasting and great destruction!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moab is destroyed; her little ones have caused a cry to be heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For by the ascent of Luhith continual weeping shall go up; for in the descent of Horonaim is heard the anguish of the cry of destruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Flee, save your lives, and be like a shrub in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For because thou hast confided in thy works and in thy treasures, thou also shalt be taken, and Chemosh shall go forth into captivity, his priests and his princes together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the waster shall come upon every city, that not a city shall escape; and the valley shall perish, and the plateau shall be destroyed: as Jehovah hath said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give wings unto Moab, that she may flee and get away; and the cities thereof shall become a desolation, without inhabitant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cursed be he that doeth the work of Jehovah negligently, and cursed be he that keepeth back his sword from blood!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moab hath been at ease from his youth, and hath settled on his lees; he hath not been emptied from vessel to vessel, neither hath he gone into captivity: therefore his taste hath remained in him, and his scent is not changed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore behold, days come, saith Jehovah, that I will send unto him pourers that shall pour him off, and shall empty his vessels, and break in pieces his flagons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh, as the house of Israel was ashamed of Bethel, their confidence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How do ye say, We are mighty, and men of valour for the war?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moab is laid waste, and his cities are gone up [in smoke], and his chosen young men are gone down to the slaughter, saith the King, whose name is Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The calamity of Moab is near to come, and his affliction hasteth fast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All ye that are about him, bemoan him; and all ye that know his name, say, How is the strong staff broken, the beautiful rod!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come down from [thy] glory and sit in the drought, O inhabitress, daughter of Dibon; the spoiler of Moab is come up against thee, thy strongholds hath he destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Stand by the way, and watch, inhabitress of Aroer; ask him that fleeth, and her that escapeth; say, What is done?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moab is put to shame; for he is broken down: howl and cry; tell it in Arnon, that Moab is laid waste.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And judgment is come upon the country of the plateau; upon Holon, and upon Jahzah, and upon Mephaath;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and upon Dibon, and upon Nebo, and upon Beth-diblathaim;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and upon Kirjathaim, and upon Beth-gamul, and upon Beth-meon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and upon Kerijoth, and upon Bozrah, and upon all the cities of the land of Moab, far and near.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make him drunken, for he magnified himself against Jehovah; and Moab shall wallow in his vomit, and he also shall be in derision.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For was not Israel a derision unto thee? Was he found among thieves, that as oft as thou didst speak of him, thou didst shake the head?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Leave the cities, and dwell in the rocks, ye inhabitants of Moab, and be like the dove, that maketh her nest in the sides of the cave's mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have heard of the arrogance of Moab, — [he is] very proud; — his loftiness, and his arrogance, and his pride, and the haughtiness of his heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I know his wrath, saith Jehovah; his pratings are vain: they do not as [they say].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore will I howl for Moab, and I will cry out for all Moab: for the men of Kir-heres shall there be moaning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O vine of Sibmah, I will weep for thee with more than the weeping of Jaazer: thy shoots passed over the sea, they reached to the sea of Jaazer. The spoiler is fallen upon thy summer fruits and upon thy vintage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And joy and gladness is taken away from the fruitful field and from the land of Moab; and I have caused wine to fail from the winepresses: they shall no more tread with shouting; the shouting shall be no shouting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of the cry from Heshbon, unto Elaleh, unto Jahaz have they uttered their voice, from Zoar unto Horonaim, [unto] Eglath-shelishijah: for even the waters of Nimrim shall become desolations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will cause to cease in Moab, saith Jehovah, him that offereth in the high place, and him that burneth incense to hisgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore my heart shall sound for Moab like pipes, and my heart shall sound like pipes for the men of Kir-heres; because the abundance that he hath gotten is perished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every head is bald, and every beard clipped; upon all the hands are cuttings, and upon the loins sackcloth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is wholly lamentation upon all the housetops of Moab, and in the public places thereof; for I have broken Moab, like a vessel wherein is no pleasure, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They howl, How is it broken down! how hath Moab turned the back with shame! And Moab shall be a derision and a terror to all that are round about him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: Behold, he shall fly as an eagle, and shall spread forth his wings over Moab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Kerijoth is taken, and the strongholds are seized, and at that day the heart of the mighty men of Moab shall be as the heart of a woman in her pangs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moab shall be destroyed from being a people, because he hath magnified himself against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear, and the pit, and the snare shall be upon thee, O inhabitant of Moab, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that fleeth from the fear shall fall into the pit; and he that getteth up out of the pit shall be taken in the snare: for I will bring upon her, upon Moab, the year of their visitation, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that fled stood under the shadow of Heshbon powerless; for a fire hath come forth from Heshbon, and a flame from the midst of Sihon, and hath consumed the corner of Moab, and the crown of the head of the sons of tumult.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to thee, Moab! The people of Chemosh is undone; for thy sons are taken away in captivity, and thy daughters are captives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I will turn the captivity of Moab at the end of the days, saith Jehovah. Thus far is the judgement of Moab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Concerning the children of Ammon. Thus saith Jehovah: Hath Israel no sons? hath he no heir? Why is Malcam heir of Gad, and his people dwell in the cities thereof?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore behold, days come, saith Jehovah, that I will cause the clamour of war to be heard in Rabbah of the children of Ammon; and it shall be a desolate heap; and her towns shall be burned with fire; and Israel shall possess them that possessed him, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Howl, Heshbon! for Ai is laid waste; cry, daughters of Rabbah, gird you with sackcloth, lament and run to and fro within the enclosures: for Malcam shall go into captivity, his priests and his princes together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore gloriest thou in the valleys? Thy valley shall flow down, O backsliding daughter, that trusteth in her treasures, [saying,] Who shall come against me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will bring a fear upon thee, saith the Lord Jehovah of hosts, from all that are about thee; and ye shall be driven out every man right forth; and none shall assemble the fugitives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards I will turn the captivity of the children of Ammon, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Concerning Edom. Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Is there no more wisdom in Teman? is counsel perished from the prudent? is their wisdom spent?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Flee, turn back, dwell deep down, ye inhabitants of Dedan! For I will bring the calamity of Esau upon him, the time that I visit him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If grape-gatherers had come to thee, would they not have left a gleaning? If thieves by night, they would destroy only till they had enough.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I have made Esau bare, I have uncovered his secret places, that he is unable to hide himself: his seed is wasted, and his brethren, and his neighbours, and he is not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Leave thine orphans, I will preserve them alive; and let thy widows trust in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah: Behold, they whose judgment was not to drink of the cup shall assuredly drink; and thou indeed, shouldest thou be altogether unpunished? Thou shalt not go unpunished, but thou shalt surely drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have sworn by myself, saith Jehovah, that Bozrah shall become an astonishment, a reproach, a waste, and a curse; and all the cities thereof shall be perpetual wastes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have heard a rumour from Jehovah, and an ambassador is sent among the nations: — Gather yourselves together, and come against her and rise up for the battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, I have made thee small among the nations, despised among men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy terribleness, the pride of thy heart, hath deceived thee, thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rock, that holdest the height of the hill. Though thou shouldest make thy nest high as the eagle, I will bring thee down from thence, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Edom shall be an astonishment: every one that goeth by it shall be astonished, and shall hiss, because of all the plagues thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As in the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, and their neighbour cities, saith Jehovah, no one shall dwell there, neither shall a son of man sojourn therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he shall come up like a lion from the swelling of the Jordan against the strong habitation; for I will make them suddenly run away from it; and who is a chosen [man] whom I shall appoint over her? For who is like me? and who will assign me a time? and who is that shepherd that will stand before me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore hear the counsel of Jehovah, which he hath taken against Edom, and his purposes which he hath purposed against the inhabitants of Teman: The little ones of the flock shall certainly draw them away; he shall certainly make their habitation desolate for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The earth quaketh at the sound of their fall; there is a cry, the sound whereof is heard in the Red sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he shall come up, and fly as an eagle, and spread forth his wings against Bozrah; and at that day the heart of the mighty men of Edom shall be as the heart of a woman in her pangs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Concerning Damascus. Hamath is put to shame, and Arpad; for they have heard evil tidings, they are melted away: there is distress on the sea; it cannot be quiet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Damascus is grown feeble: she turneth herself to flee, and terror hath seized on her; trouble and sorrows have taken hold of her as of a woman in travail.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How is not the town of praise forsaken, the city of my joy!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore shall her young men fall in her streets, and all the men of war be cut off in that day, saith Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will kindle a fire in the wall of Damascus, and it shall consume the palaces of Ben-Hadad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Concerning Kedar, and concerning the kingdoms of Hazor, which Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon smote. Thus saith Jehovah: Arise, go up to Kedar, and spoil the men of the east.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their tents and their flocks shall they take; their curtains and all their vessels, and their camels, shall they carry away for themselves; and they shall cry unto them, Terror on every side!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Flee, wander very far, dwell deep down, ye inhabitants of Hazor, saith Jehovah; for Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon hath taken counsel against you, and hath conceived a purpose against you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, get you up against the nation at ease, that dwelleth securely, saith Jehovah, which hath neither gates nor bars: they dwell alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their camels shall be a booty, and the multitude of their cattle a spoil; and I will scatter to every wind them that have the corners [of their beard] cut off, and I will bring their calamity from all sides thereof, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hazor shall be a dwelling-place of jackals, a desolation for ever. No one shall dwell there, neither shall a son of man sojourn therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word of Jehovah that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam, in the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Behold, I will break the bow of Elam, the chief of their might.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And upon Elam will I bring the four winds, from the four ends of the heavens, and I will scatter them toward all those winds; and there shall be no nation whither the outcasts of Elam shall not come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will cause Elam to be dismayed before their enemies, and before them that seek their life; and I will bring evil upon them, my fierce anger, saith Jehovah; and I will send the sword after them, till I have consumed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will set my throne in Elam, and will destroy from thence king and princes, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But it shall come to pass at the end of the days, I will turn the captivity of Elam, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that Jehovah spoke concerning Babylon, concerning the land of the Chaldeans, through Jeremiah the prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Declare ye among the nations, and publish, and lift up a banner; publish, conceal not! Say, Babylon is taken, Bel is put to shame, Merodach is dismayed: her images are put to shame, her idols are dismayed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For out of the north there cometh up a nation against her, which shall make her land desolate, and none shall dwell therein: both man and beast are fled; they are gone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days, and at that time, saith Jehovah, the children of Israel shall come, they and the children of Judah together, going and weeping as they go, and shall seek Jehovah theirGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall inquire concerning Zion, with their faces thitherward, [saying,] Come, and let us join ourselves to Jehovah, in an everlasting covenant that shall not be forgotten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My people are lost sheep; their shepherds have caused them to go astray, they turned them away on the mountains: they went from mountain to hill, they forgot their resting-place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that found them devoured them, and their adversaries said, We are not guilty, because they have sinned against Jehovah, the habitation of righteousness, even Jehovah, the hope of their fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Flee out of the midst of Babylon, and go forth out of the land of the Chaldeans, and be as the he-goats before the flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, I will raise and cause to come up against Babylon, an assemblage of great nations from the north country; and they shall set themselves in array against her: from thence shall she be taken. Their arrows shall be as those of a mighty expert man: none shall return empty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Chaldea shall be a spoil: all the spoilers thereof shall be satiated, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye rejoiced, for ye triumphed, ye plunderers of my heritage; for ye have been wanton as the heifer at grass, and neighed as steeds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your mother hath been sorely put to shame; she that bore you hath been covered with reproach: behold, [she is become] hindmost of the nations, a wilderness, a dry land, and a desert.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of the wrath of Jehovah, it shall not be inhabited, but it shall be wholly desolate; every one that goeth by Babylon shall be astonished, and shall hiss, because of all her plagues.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Put yourselves in array against Babylon round about, all ye that bend the bow; shoot at her, spare no arrows: for she hath sinned against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shout against her round about: she hath given her hand; her ramparts are fallen, her walls are thrown down: for this is the vengeance of Jehovah. Take vengeance upon her; as she hath done, do unto her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cut off the sower from Babylon, and him that handleth the sickle in the time of harvest. For fear of the oppressing sword let them turn every one to his people, and let them flee every one to his own land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Israel is a hunted sheep; the lions have driven him away: first the king of Assyria devoured him, and last this Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon hath broken his bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: Behold, I will visit the king of Babylon and his land, like as I have visited the king of Assyria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will bring Israel again to his pasture, and he shall feed on Carmel and Bashan, and his soul shall be satisfied upon mount Ephraim and in Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days, and at that time, saith Jehovah, the iniquity of Israel shall be sought for, and there shall be none; and the sins of Judah, and they shall not be found: for I will pardon those whom I leave remaining.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go up against the land of double rebellion, against it, and against the inhabitants of visitation; waste and utterly destroy after them, saith Jehovah, and do according to all that I have commanded thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A sound of battle is in the land, and great destruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How is the hammer of the whole earth cut asunder and broken! How is Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have laid a snare for thee, and thou, Babylon, art also taken, and thou wast not aware; thou art found, and also caught, for thou hast contended with Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath opened his armoury, and hath brought forth the weapons of his indignation; for this is a work for the Lord, Jehovah of hosts, in the land of the Chaldeans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come ye against her from every quarter, open her storehouses; pile her up like sheaves, and destroy her utterly: let nothing of her be left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Slay all her bullocks; let them go down to the slaughter: woe unto them! for their day is come, the time of their visitation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of them that flee and escape out of the land of Babylon, to declare in Zion the vengeance of Jehovah ourGod, the vengeance of his temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Call together the archers against Babylon, all those that bend the bow: encamp against her round about; let there be no escaping: recompense her according to her work; according to all that she hath done, do unto her: for she hath acted proudly against Jehovah, against the Holy One of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore shall her young men fall in her streets; and all her men of war shall be cut off in that day, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I am against thee, proud one, saith the Lord Jehovah of hosts; for thy day is come, the time that I visit thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the proud one shall stumble and fall, and none shall raise him up; yea, I will kindle a fire in his cities, and it shall devour all that are round about him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: The children of Israel and the children of Judah were together oppressed; and all that took them captives held them fast: they refused to let them go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their Redeemer is strong; Jehovah of hosts is his name: he will thoroughly plead their cause, that he may give rest to the land, and disquiet the inhabitants of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sword is upon the Chaldeans, saith Jehovah, and upon the inhabitants of Babylon, and upon her princes, and upon her wise men;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the sword is upon the liars, and they shall become fools; the sword is upon her mighty men, and they shall be dismayed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the sword is upon their horses, and upon their chariots, and upon all the mingled people that are in the midst of her, and they shall become as women; the sword is upon her treasures, and they shall be robbed:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a drought is upon her waters, and they shall be dried up; for it is a land of graven images, and they are mad after frightful idols.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore wild beasts of the desert with jackals shall dwell there, and ostriches shall dwell therein; and it shall be no more inhabited for ever, neither shall it be dwelt in from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As whenGod overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah, and their neighbour cities, saith Jehovah, no one shall dwell there, neither shall a son of man sojourn therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, a people cometh from the north, and a great nation. And many kings shall arise from the uttermost parts of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They lay hold of bow and spear; they are cruel, and will not shew mercy; their voice roareth like the sea, and they ride upon horses — set in array like a man for the battle, against thee, O daughter of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The king of Babylon hath heard the report of them, and his hands wax feeble; trouble hath taken hold of him, pangs as of a woman in travail.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he shall come up like a lion from the swelling of the Jordan against the strong habitation; for I will make him suddenly run away from it; and who is a chosen [man] whom I may appoint over her? For who is like me? and who will assign me a time? and who is that shepherd that will stand before me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore hear the counsel of Jehovah, which he hath taken against Babylon, and his purposes which he hath purposed against the land of the Chaldeans: The little ones of the flock shall certainly draw them away; he shall certainly make their habitation desolate for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the sound of the taking of Babylon the earth hath quaked, and the cry is heard among the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will raise up against Babylon, and against them that dwell in the heart of those that rise against me, a destroying wind;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will send unto Babylon strangers, who shall fan her, and shall empty her land: for in the day of trouble they shall be against her round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Against him that bendeth let the archer bend his bow, and against him that lifteth himself up in his coat of mail; and spare not her young men: destroy utterly all her host.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the slain shall fall in the land of the Chaldeans, and they that are thrust through in her streets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Israel is not forsaken, nor Judah of hisGod, of Jehovah of hosts; for their land is full of guilt against the Holy One of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Flee out of the midst of Babylon, and save every man his life; be ye not cut off in her iniquity: for this is the time of Jehovah's vengeance: he shall render unto her a recompence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Babylon hath been a golden cup in Jehovah's hand, that made all the earth drunken: the nations have drunk of her wine; therefore have the nations become mad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Babylon is suddenly fallen and ruined. Howl over her; take balm for her pain, if so be she may be healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have treated Babylon, but she is not healed: forsake her, and let us go every one into his own country; for her judgment reacheth unto the heavens, and is lifted up to the skies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath brought forth our righteousness: come, and let us declare in Zion the work of Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sharpen the arrows; take the shields. Jehovah hath stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes; for his purpose is against Babylon, to destroy it; for this is the vengeance of Jehovah, the vengeance of his temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift up a banner towards the walls of Babylon, make the watch strong, set the watchmen, prepare the ambushes; for Jehovah hath both devised and done that which he spoke against the inhabitants of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou that dwellest upon many waters, abundant in treasures, thine end is come, the measure of thy rapacity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah of hosts hath sworn by himself, I will assuredly fill thee with men, as with caterpillars; and they shall raise a shout against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heavens by his understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When he uttereth his voice, there is a tumult of waters in the heavens, and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the end of the earth; he maketh lightnings for the rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every man is become brutish, so as to have no knowledge; every founder is put to shame by the graven image, for his molten image is falsehood, and there is no breath in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are vanity, a work of delusion: in the time of their visitation they shall perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The portion of Jacob is not like them; for it is he that hath formed all things: and [Israel] is the rod of his inheritance: Jehovah of hosts is his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou art my maul, [my] weapons of war: and with thee I will break in pieces the nations, and I will with thee destroy kingdoms;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with thee I will break in pieces the horse and his rider; and with thee I will break in pieces the chariot and its driver;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with thee will I break in pieces man and woman; and with thee will I break in pieces old and young; and with thee will I break in pieces the young man and the maid;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with thee will I break in pieces the shepherd and his flock; and with thee will I break in pieces the husbandman and his yoke of oxen; and with thee will I break in pieces governors and rulers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will render unto Babylon and to all the inhabitants of Chaldea, in your sight, all their evil which they have done in Zion, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I am against thee, mount of destruction, saith Jehovah, which destroyest all the earth; and I will stretch out my hand upon thee, and roll thee down from the rocks, and will make thee a burning mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall not take of thee a stone for a corner, nor a stone for foundations; for thou shalt be desolate for ever, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift up a banner in the land, blow the trumpet among the nations, prepare nations against her; call together against her the kingdoms of Ararat, Minni, and Ashkenaz; appoint a captain against her; cause the horses to come up as the bristly caterpillars.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Prepare nations against her, the kings of the Medes, their governors and all their rulers, yea, all the land of their dominion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the land trembleth and is in pain; for the purposes of Jehovah against Babylon do stand, to make the land of Babylon a desolation, without inhabitant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mighty men of Babylon have ceased to fight, they are sitting in the fortresses; their might hath failed, they are become as women: they have set her dwelling places on fire; her bars are broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Courier runneth to meet courier, and messenger to meet messenger, to announce to the king of Babylon that his city is taken from end to end;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the passages are seized, and the reedy places are burnt with fire, and the men of war are affrighted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel: The daughter of Babylon is like a threshing-floor, at the time of its being trodden; yet a little while, and the time of harvest shall come for her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon hath devoured me, he hath crushed me, he hath made me an empty vessel; he hath swallowed me up like a dragon, he hath filled his belly with my delicates, he hath cast me out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon, shall the inhabitress of Zion say; and, My blood be upon the inhabitants of Chaldea, shall Jerusalem say.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Behold, I will plead thy cause, and take vengeance for thee; and I will dry up her sea, and make her spring dry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Babylon shall become heaps, a dwelling-place of jackals, an astonishment, and a hissing, without inhabitant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall roar together like young lions, growl as lions' whelps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When they are heated, I will prepare their drink, and I will make them drunken, that they may exult, and sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams with he-goats.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How is Sheshach taken! and how is the praise of the whole earth seized! How is Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sea is come up upon Babylon: she is covered with the multitude of its waves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her cities are become a desolation, a dry land, and a desert, a land wherein no one dwelleth, neither doth a son of man pass thereby.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will punish Bel in Babylon, and I will bring forth out of his mouth what he hath swallowed up; and the nations shall not flow together any more unto him: yea, the wall of Babylon is fallen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go ye out of the midst of her, my people, and deliver every man his soul from the fierce anger of Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest your heart faint, and ye fear for the rumour that shall be heard in the land; for a rumour shall come [one] year, and after that a rumour in [another] year, and violence in the earth, ruler against ruler.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore behold, days are coming when I will punish the graven images of Babylon; and her whole land shall be put to shame, and all her slain shall fall in the midst of her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the heavens and the earth, and all that is therein, shall shout aloud over Babylon; for out of the north the spoilers shall come against her, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As Babylon caused the slain of Israel to fall, so at Babylon shall fall the slain of all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye that have escaped the sword, go, stand not still: remember Jehovah from afar, and let Jerusalem come into your mind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — We are put to shame, for we have heard reproach; confusion hath covered our face: for strangers are come into the sanctuaries of Jehovah's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Therefore behold, days come, saith Jehovah, that I will punish her graven images; and throughout her land the wounded shall groan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though Babylon should mount up to the heavens, and though she should fortify the height of her strength, yet from me shall spoilers come unto her, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sound of a cry [cometh] from Babylon, and great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Jehovah spoileth Babylon, and he will destroy out of her the great voice; and their waves roar like great waters, the noise of their voice resoundeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the spoiler is come against her, against Babylon, and her mighty men are taken; their bows are broken in pieces; for Jehovah, theGod of recompences, will certainly requite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will make drunk her princes, and her wise men, her governors, and her rulers, and her mighty men; and they shall sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, saith the King, whose name is Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: The broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly laid bare, and her high gates shall be burned with fire; so that the peoples will have laboured in vain, and the nations for the fire: and they shall be weary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Nerijah, the son of Mahseiah, when he went with Zedekiah the king of Judah to Babylon in the fourth year of his reign. Now Seraiah was chief chamberlain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come upon Babylon, all these words that are written against Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeremiah said to Seraiah, When thou comest to Babylon, see that thou read all these words;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and say, Jehovah, thou hast spoken concerning this place, that thou wilt cut it off, so that none shall dwell in it, neither man nor beast, but that it shall be desolate for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, when thou hast ended reading this book, [that] thou shalt bind a stone to it, and cast it into the midst of the Euphrates;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shalt say, Thus shall Babylon sink, and shall not rise, because of the evil that I will bring upon it: and they shall be weary. Thus far the words of Jeremiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign; and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Hamutal, daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah, according to all that Jehoiakim had done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For, because the anger of Jehovah was against Jerusalem and Judah, until he had cast them out from his presence, Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth of the month, [that] Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon came, he and all his army, against Jerusalem; and they encamped against it, and built turrets against it round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the city was besieged until the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the fourth month, on the ninth of the month, the famine prevailed in the city, and there was no bread for the people of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the city was broken into: and all the men of war fled, and went forth out of the city by night, by the way of the gate between the two walls, which was by the king's garden (now the Chaldeans were near the city round about); and they went the way toward the plain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king, and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and all his army was scattered from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took the king, and brought him up to the king of Babylon, unto Riblah in the land of Hamath, and he pronounced judgment upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes; and he slaughtered also all the princes of Judah in Riblah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put out the eyes of Zedekiah, and bound him with chains of brass; and the king of Babylon carried him to Babylon, and put him in prison till the day of his death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the fifth month, on the tenth of the month, which was in the nineteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, Nebuzar-adan, captain of the body-guard, who stood before the king of Babylon, came unto Jerusalem;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he burned the house of Jehovah, and the king's house, and all the houses of Jerusalem; and every great [man's] house he burned with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the army of the Chaldeans, that were with the captain of the body-guard, broke down all the walls of Jerusalem round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard carried away captive of the poorest sort of the people, and the rest of the people that were left in the city, and the deserters that had deserted to the king of Babylon, and the rest of the multitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard left of the poor of the land for vinedressers and for husbandmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the brazen pillars that were in the house of Jehovah, and the bases, and the brazen sea that was in the house of Jehovah, the Chaldeans broke up, and carried all the brass thereof to Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The pots also, and the shovels, and the knives, and the bowls, and the cups, and all the vessels of brass wherewith they ministered, they took away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the basons and the censers, and the bowls, and the pots, and the candlesticks, and the cups, and the goblets, that which was of gold in gold, and that which was of silver in silver, the captain of the body-guard took away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The two pillars, the one sea, and the twelve brazen oxen that formed the bases, which king Solomon had made for the house of Jehovah: for the brass of all these vessels there was no weight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as to the pillars: the height of one pillar was eighteen cubits, and a line of twelve cubits encompassed it; and the thickness thereof was four fingers: it was hollow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the capital upon it was brass, and the height of the one capital [was] five cubits; and the network and the pomegranates, upon the capital round about, all of brass; and similarly for the second pillar, and the pomegranates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were ninety-six pomegranates on the [four] sides; all the pomegranates upon the network were a hundred round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the captain of the body-guard took Seraiah the chief priest, and Zephaniah the second priest, and the three doorkeepers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And out of the city he took a eunuch that was set over the men of war, and seven men of them that were in the king's presence, who were found in the city, and the scribe of the captain of the host, who enrolled the people of the land. And sixty men of the people of the land that were found in the midst of the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard took them, and brought them to the king of Babylon to Riblah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the king of Babylon smote them, and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was carried away captive out of his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the people whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year three thousand and twenty-three Jews;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar [he carried away captive] from Jerusalem eight hundred and thirty-two persons;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadrezzar, Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard carried away captive of the Jews seven hundred and forty-five persons: all the persons were four thousand six hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth of the month, [that] Evil-Merodach king of Babylon, in the year that he began to reign, lifted up the head of Jehoiachin king of Judah, and brought him forth out of prison;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he spoke kindly unto him, and set his seat above the seat of the kings that were with him in Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he changed his prison garments; and he ate bread before him continually all the days of his life;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his allowance was a continual allowance given him by the king of Babylon, every day a portion until the day of his death, all the days of his life.
